issue_title: Raise exception if `request.tm` is accessed for request opted out by `activate_hook`
issue_body:
If you use an `activate_hook` to opt certain requests out of the transaction manager tween, it is currently still possible to get a reference to the request transaction manager using `request.tm`. 

This isn't usually a big problem, but if instead of using a threadlocal transaction manager, you are using a per-request tm, say

```python
config.add_settings({
   "tm.manager_hook": lambda request: transaction.TransactionManager(),
})
```

and then registering the session with the transaction manager using `zope.sqlalchemy.register(session, transaction_manager=request.tm)`, then it's possible to end up in a situation where the session is registered with the transaction manager but the transaction manager isn't hooked up to the request lifecycle.

This wouldn't be the end of the world, but it results in references to `id(session)` not being cleaned up from [the `_SESSION_STATE` threadlocal](https://github.com/zopefoundation/zope.sqlalchemy/blob/9b70ff1cdbebb328ed287183112b696c1184ec61/src/zope/sqlalchemy/datamanager.py#L48) in `zope.sqlalchemy`.

In an environment where a new `Session` instance is created for each request, this results in a (small) memory leak. Much more importantly, it is very easy to get into a situation where a brand-new session is created with an `id(session)` that's already in `_SESSION_STATE`, resulting in `zope.sqlalchemy.register` silently failing to register the session with the transaction manager.

Of course, part of the problem here is that of usage: why would you call `zope.sqlalchemy.register(...)` in a request that you opted out of the transaction manager machinery. But I'd argue that this is a bit of a footgun, and `pyramid_tm` could prevent this class of bug entirely if the `tm` request property raised an `AttributeError` (or a custom exception type) when used in the context of a request for which `activate_hook` returned False.

I'm happy to put together a PR to implement this behaviour, but thought I should open this issue for feedback on the idea before I did so. Thoughts?

issue_title: Can't pass environment variables to Git.execute
issue_body:
This is needed for commands like `git-commit` and `git-commit-tree` in order to be able to specify author and committer information. While `git-commit` does provide the `--author` and `--date` to specify author information, there are no options for specifying committer information, and `git-commit-tree` is wholly dependent on environment variables.

For instance, the following code fails with `TypeError: type object got multiple values for keyword argument 'env'`.

```
def pipe_of(string):
	import os
	if type(string) is str:
		string = bytes(string, 'utf-8')
	r,w = os.pipe()
	os.write(w, string)
	os.close(w)
	return r
git = Repo('/path/ro/repo').git
blob = git.execute(['git', 'hash-object', '-w', '--stdin'], istream=pipe_of("my data"))
tree = git.execute(['git', 'mktree'], istream=pipe_of("100644 blob {}\tmy-file\n".format(blob)))
commit = git.execute(['git', 'commit-tree', tree], istream=None, env={
	'GIT_AUTHOR_NAME':'Author Name',
	'GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL':'author@example.com',
	'GIT_AUTHOR_DATE':'1400000000+0000',
	'GIT_COMMITTER_NAME':'Comitter Name',
	'GIT_COMMITTER_EMAIL':'committer@example.com',
	'GIT_COMMITTER_DATE':'1500000000+0000',
	})
```

issue_title: Send email through smtp in superlance using crashmail
issue_body:
Hi,

I've posted this question on stackoverflow but given there isn't a lot of questions about superlance, I decided to repost the question here.

I'm trying to set up the email sending when a process changes state in [supervisord][1] by using [crashmail][2]. Having no luck with the default `sendmail` program which requires quite a lot of setup, I decided to go with a small script in Python that sends email using SMTP.

This worked very well (I received indeed an email saying that the process state changes) for the first state change but stop working afterward. I have tried to change different options in `supervisord` such as `buffer_size` or `autorestart` but it has no effect.

Here is the script I use to trigger the `supervisord` state changes:

    import time

    from datetime import datetime

    if __name__ == '__main__':
        print(">>>>> STARTING ...", flush=True)
        while True:
            print("sleep now:", datetime.utcnow(), flush=True)
            time.sleep(30)
            raise Exception("meo meo")

This is the script that sends email through Gmail. This one will send the `stdin`.

    #!/usr/bin/env python
    
    import smtplib
    
    
    def get_server():
        smtpserver = smtplib.SMTP('smtp.gmail.com:587')
        smtpserver.ehlo()
        smtpserver.starttls()
        smtpserver.login("user@gmail.com", "password")
        return smtpserver
    
    
    if __name__ == '__main__':
        import sys
    
        data = sys.stdin.read()
    
        s = get_server()
        s.sendmail('from@gmail.com', ['myemail@fitle.com'], data)
        s.quit()
   
Here is my `supervisord.conf`

	[eventlistener:crashmail]
	command=crashmail -a -m myemail@gmail.com -s /home/ubuntu/mysendmail.py
	events=PROCESS_STATE
	buffer_size=102400
	autorestart=true


Does anyone have any idea why? 
Thanks!


  [1]: http://supervisord.org/
  [2]: https://superlance.readthedocs.org/en/latest/crashmail.html

issue_title: Project Metadata in Pipfile (name, version, license, ...)
issue_body:
NOTE: I am including the request here and not sending it to the wiki because it current says that the wiki is not up.

##### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.

First of all, I really like how Pipenv simulates much of the functionality of npm / yarn and package.json files that are used in Node.js development. It is really clean, simple, and consistent. One thing that I find frustrating with my current Pipenv setup for developing libraries is having to manage a separate setup.py file for publishing to PyPi.

##### Describe the solution you'd like

As I mentioned above, I think that (like in the Node.js toolchain), metadata about the project should be stored in the Pipfile and initialized by pipenv. Using a command like `pipenv init`, users can input optional values for fields like name, version, license, description, and so on. They can also use `pipenv init -y` to fill in default values or something similar.

In addition, with this information, it would be possible to generate a setup.py file using a command like `pipenv generate setup.py` or something similar. This command would take the metadata and package dependencies and output a `setup.py`. This command can be integrated in a CI toolchain if desired.

##### Describe alternatives you've considered

Of course, the current alternative is to manage a setup.py which contains the metadata about the project. This is the current base method, which in some ways makes more sense, but is also harder to manage.

Benefits:
First of all, a lot of the metadata that I am proposing is only necessary when developing libraries and not applications. So if someone uses pipenv in their application, they may not find this feature useful. Of course, they can choose not to include that information in the Pipfile or skip the fields in `pipenv init`. Also, application developers have no need for a setup.py, so the feature is not necessary for them.

Drawbacks:
For package developers, this setup adds a lot of extra complexity. They have to manually create a `setup.py` file with the metadata and a list of package dependencies. So as they add packages in pipenv, they would have to manually add them in the `setup.py`. 

##### Additional context

In order to include the metadata within the Pipfile, it must be placed in a current section or a new section called info or something. Therefore, the Pipfile specification would also have to changed, or pipenv would not follow the standard. This would definitely be a problem, but I am not sure how important it is to follow the Pipfile standard as it is currently under active design. It also seems like pipenv currently doesn't exactly follow the standard; I don't think Pipfiles should include the 'scripts' section even though pipenv currently supports it.

I would love to help develop this feature and make a PR, but I would like other's opinions before continuing.

issue_title: Pipenv ignores conflicts between default and development dependencies
issue_body:
##### Describe your environment

1. OS Type: OS X
1. Python version: `Python 3.6.3`
1. Pipenv version: `pipenv, version 8.3.1`

##### Expected result

Suppose I have a default dependency that depends on `six==1.10.0` and a development dependency that depends on `six==1.11.0`.

I expect to get some sort of error here, because it's impossible to satisfy both transitive requirements simultaneously.

##### Actual result

```
$ pipenv graph
dep-bar==0.0.0
  - six [required: ==1.11.0, installed: 1.10.0]
dep-foo==0.0.0
  - six [required: ==1.10.0, installed: 1.10.0]
```

`Pipfile.lock` is great, too, featuring:

```
    "default": {
        "dep-foo": {
            "editable": true,
            "git": "git@github.com:taion/dep_foo.git"
        },
        "six": {
            "hashes": [
                "sha256:0ff78c403d9bccf5a425a6d31a12aa6b47f1c21ca4dc2573a7e2f32a97335eb1",
                "sha256:105f8d68616f8248e24bf0e9372ef04d3cc10104f1980f54d57b2ce73a5ad56a"
            ],
            "version": "==1.10.0"
        }
    },
    "develop": {
        "dep-bar": {
            "editable": true,
            "git": "git@github.com:taion/dep_bar.git"
        },
        "six": {
            "hashes": [
                "sha256:832dc0e10feb1aa2c68dcc57dbb658f1c7e65b9b61af69048abc87a2db00a0eb",
                "sha256:70e8a77beed4562e7f14fe23a786b54f6296e34344c23bc42f07b15018ff98e9"
            ],
            "version": "==1.11.0"
        }
    }
```

##### Steps to replicate

```
[[source]]

url = "https://pypi.python.org/simple"
verify_ssl = true
name = "pypi"


[packages]

dep_foo = {git = "git@github.com:taion/dep_foo.git", editable = true}


[dev-packages]

dep_bar = {git = "git@github.com:taion/dep_bar.git", editable = true}
```

issue_title: Import / include settings from other files by reference
issue_body:
Putting common settings into a separate file and including them by reference can simplify config files, aid in clarity, and improve reusability.

A potential format might be e.g.

```toml
# Include everything
[[include]]
source = "../UseEverything"

# Include only specific parts
[[include]]
source = "../OnlyRequires"
parts = ['requires']

# Link from the Internet
[[include]]
source = "https://example.com/someInternetSettings"
sha256 = "edeaaff3f1774ad2888673770c6d64097e391bc362d7d6fb34982ddf0efd18cb"
```

issue_title: Question: how to point auditwheel to the correct library path
issue_body:
I receive the following error:

`ValueError: Cannot repair wheel, because required library "libarb.so.2" could not be located`

However, I know where this library is located. How can I point auditwheel to that location?

For one of the projects I work on (Astropy) we have been making use of code that can be simplified to:

```python
import pkg_resources
from setuptools import Distribution


class CustomDistribution(Distribution):
    def get_option_dict(self, command_name):
        opts = Distribution.get_option_dict(self, command_name)
        if command_name == 'easy_install':
            opts['find_links'] = ('setup script', ['astropy-helpers-2.0.3.tar.gz'])
            opts['allow_hosts'] = ('setup script', '')
        return opts


CustomDistribution(attrs={'setup_requires': ['astropy-helpers']})

print(pkg_resources.working_set.by_key.get('astropy-helpers'))
```

This requires the following tar file to run: [astropy-helpers-2.0.3.tar.gz](https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/46/49/4882dc7b3fc1c47d481db229777a82e74751e6a1f0a4af90d9df5285bb21/astropy-helpers-2.0.3.tar.gz)

Before https://github.com/pypa/setuptools/pull/1150, this would cause astropy-helpers to be installed to the ``.eggs`` directory using the local tar file, but since the changes in https://github.com/pypa/setuptools/pull/1150 this no longer works (in the above example, the last line used to return a distribution object, but astropy-helpers is now no longer in the ``working_set`` so this returns ``None``)

Is this an unintended consequence of https://github.com/pypa/setuptools/pull/1150, or should we update our code above for it to continue working?

cc @benoit-pierre @jaraco

issue_title: msvc9_query_vcvarsall returns unicode strings on Python 2.7 64-bit and regular strings on 32-bit
issue_body:
msvc9_query_vcvarsall returns environment dict with keys "lib", "libpath", "include" and "PATH". On Python 2.7 32-bit values of this dict are regular strings, however on Python 2.7 64-bit this dict contains unicode strings. So when I later use this dict to update os.environ it throws an error:

```
TypeError: environment can only contain string
```

issue_title: Move requirement files to a separated directory
issue_body: Let's move the requirement files to a separate directory to keep them organized.

The documentation of `treq.testing.RequestSequence` says to use `logger.error` to report failures asynchronously, but this doesn't actually work because `RequestSequence` calls that function with a string. [Trial only fails the test if the log message has an associated Failure](https://twistedmatrix.com/trac/ticket/9188), which is not the case here.

We should consider making this argument optional and making `RequestSequence` log an error directly, rather than forcing the caller to pass a callback.

issue_title: Pipe symbol ("|") is not percent encoded
issue_body:
Pipe symbol ("|") is in reserved symbols list in url.py [https://github.com/scrapy/w3lib/blob/master/w3lib/url.py#L67](url) and is not percent encoded by safe_url_string which used by scrapy to download urls. 
RFC mentioned in url.py [https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc3986.txt](url) doesn't contain "|" in reserved symbols: 

```
      reserved    = gen-delims / sub-delims

      gen-delims  = ":" / "/" / "?" / "#" / "[" / "]" / "@"

      sub-delims  = "!" / "$" / "&" / "'" / "(" / ")"
                  / "*" / "+" / "," / ";" / "="

```
And I've found a site (in top 20 of Alexa, possible using play framework) which has such links with pipes, and it is answering with http code 400 (bad request) if "|" is not percent encoded in url. 
Is this a bug? How can I avoid it properly? For now I just removed "|" from url.py itself.
Selectors shouldn't import from Scrapy, otherwise splitting them to a separate library doesn't provide benefits.

issue_title: Have `Tuple.values` be a `namedtuple` so fields can be accessed by name
issue_body:
This was brought up as part of our discussion of the rejected #120.  What we want to do is:

1.  Submit a PR to Storm that serializes  [`TopologyContext.componentToStreamToFields`](https://github.com/apache/storm/blob/master/storm-core/src/jvm/backtype/storm/task/TopologyContext.java#L61) and sends that along as part of Multi-Lang handshake.
2.  Add a `_stream_fields` dictionary attribute to the  `Component` class that maps from streams to `namedtuple` types representing the names of fields/values in the tuple.  This should get created at handshake time based on the contents of `componentToStreamToFields`.
3.  Modify `Component.read_tuple()` to set `Tuple.values` to be a `namedtuple` of the appropriate type for the current stream (by looking it up in `Component._stream_fields`.

issue_title: erase_in_display fails if the argument is 3
issue_body:
https://github.com/selectel/pyte/blob/master/pyte/screens.py#L700
```python
    def erase_in_display(self, how=0, private=False):
        """Erases display in a specific way.
        :param int how: defines the way the line should be erased in:
            * ``0`` -- Erases from cursor to end of screen, including
              cursor position.
            * ``1`` -- Erases from beginning of screen to cursor,
              including cursor position.
            * ``2`` -- Erases complete display. All lines are erased
              and changed to single-width. Cursor does not move.
        :param bool private: when ``True`` character attributes are left
                             unchanged **not implemented**.
        """
        if how == 0:
            # a) erase from cursor to the end of the display, including
            #    the cursor,
            interval = range(self.cursor.y + 1, self.lines)
        elif how == 1:
            # b) erase from the beginning of the display to the cursor,
            #    including it,
            interval = range(self.cursor.y)
        elif how == 2:
            # c) erase the whole display.
            interval = range(self.lines)

        for line in interval:
            self.buffer[line][:] = \
                (self.cursor.attrs for _ in range(self.columns))

        # In case of 0 or 1 we have to erase the line with the cursor.
        if how == 0 or how == 1:
            self.erase_in_line(how)
```

There is a potential bug here, which will be triggered while `how` var isn't in the list [0, 1, 2].

sqlalchemy.exc.OperationalError: (OperationalError) (1067, "Invalid default value for 'timestamp'") "\nCREATE TABLE instance_status_history (\n\tserver VARCHAR(100) DEFAULT '' NOT NULL, \n\ttimestamp TIMESTAMP DEFAULT '0000-00-00 00:00:00', \n\tstatus VARCHAR(32) DEFAULT '' NOT NULL, \n\tPRIMARY KEY (server, timestamp)\n)\n\n" ()
```

This is the first one that migration encounters. There are actually several DATETIME and TIMESTAMP columns in the migration version scripts in Taurus.Engine and HTM.IT with either `0000-00-00 00:00:00` or `0` DEFAULT values.

This is related to mysql’s wishy-washy position on the NO_ZERO_DATE flag that has gone and come again starting with 5.7.4. See "SQL Mode Changes in MySQL 5.7" http://dev.mysql.com/doc/refman/5.7/en/sql-mode.html#sql-mode-changes. Apparently, as of mysql 5.7.8, NO_ZERO_DATE is back in the default mode set. Apparently, you can get rid of the mentioned error by removing NO_ZERO_DATE from mysql’s config file (my.cnf/my.ini?). 

According to the above-mentioned mysql note, "The long term plan is still to have the three affected modes be included in strict SQL mode and to remove them as explicit modes in a future MySQL release. ", but they have been going back and forth to give affected apps additional time to modify their schema.

I think that we need to modify the affected schema to use datetime column attributes that are compatible with mysql 5.6 and 5.7.8+ and don’t break the application logic (update affected logic, if needed), in case something in the logic relies on the original default values.

We need to take into consideration the following scenarios that our users may experience with default mysql installation options (e.g., default options as installed by homebrew); remember that the user's choice of mysql version is not driven solely by numenta software, and considerations such as mysql security fixes and compatibility and features needed by their other apps are critical.
1. Installing Taurus.Engine (or HTM.IT) against MySQL 5.6 (e.g., 5.6.23) for the first time
2. Installing Taurus.Engine (or HTM.IT) against MySQL 5.7.8+ (e.g., 5.7.10) for the first time
3. Migrating (e.g., graceful-update-production-taurus-servers) Taurus.Engine (or HTM.IT) to the new version of migration scripts with fixed TIMESTAMP and DATETIME columns against MySQL 5.6 (e.g., 5.6.23)
4. Migrating (e.g., graceful-update-production-taurus-servers) Taurus.Engine (or HTM.IT) to the new version of migration scripts with fixed TIMESTAMP and DATETIME columns against MySQL 5.7.8+ (e.g., 5.7.10)
5. Original deployment against MySQL 5.6 (e.g., 5.6.23) without the new TIMESTAMP/DATETIME column definitions, then upgrade to mysql 5.7.8+ (e.g., 5.7.10), then update Taurus.Engine (same for HTM.IT) to software with new fixed TIMESTAMP/DATETIME column definitions, and perform database migration

NOTE: you may encounter what appears to be a bug http://bugs.mysql.com/bug.php?id=80163 in MySQL 5.7 (5.7.10 definitely) that affects the "annotation" table htm.it/htm/it/app/repository/migrations/versions/000_3a7e06671df4_base_to_1.6.py, where there are multiple TIMESTAMP columns without defaults. You may need to find suitable defaults to work around that issue, being careful not to break application logic that might rely on the builtin default values.

The file spatial_pooler.py contains two helper classes, CorticalColumns and BinaryCorticalColumns. These names are incorrect as these should refer to mini columns.  There are also numerous references to cortical columns in the comments.

The term "cortical column" means something completely different in neuroscience. This issue is particularly relevant since in the new theory work, we rely on the correct meaning of "cortical column".

This should be an easy change for someone to implement. Other than this file and some tests, nothing else in NuPIC uses these helper functions.

issue_title: Implement Capnp serialization configuration for TM

issue_body:
- Each HTM pipeline composes of SP and TM(TP) computation; these 2 components take approximately same time now, see https://github.com/numenta/nupic/pull/3131 

The full pipeline is `Encoder -> SP -> TM -> Classifier/Anomaly`, but the SP,TM are the most computationally intensive routines.  

- All modern systems are multithreaded

Therefore the proposed simple optimization would halve the time of practical HTM applications. 

Requirement: 
- the 2 regions should be synced on a `spOutput` variable, which is then input to TM

Implementation: 
* show a multithreaded use in #889 
* create a parallel/synced region that wraps any other region inside it
See parent #441
which it basically is, so in `nupic/bindings` it can be accessed intuitively (instead of TP10X2 ?)

issue_body:
The following cookiecutter change:

   https://github.com/inveniosoftware/cookiecutter-invenio-module/pull/98

should be propagated to this Invenio module.

Namely, in `run-tests.sh`, the sphinx for doctests is invoked after pytest run:

```console
$ tail -3 ./\{\{\ cookiecutter.project_shortname\ \}\}/run-tests.sh
sphinx-build -qnNW docs docs/_build/html && python setup.py test && sphinx-build -qnNW -b doctest docs docs/_build/doctest
```

This sometimes led to problems on Travis CI with the second sphinx-build run due
to "disappearing" dependencies after the example application was tested.

A solution that worked for invenio-marc21 (see
https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-marc21/pull/49#issuecomment-281287103)
and that was integrated in cookiecutter (see
https://github.com/inveniosoftware/cookiecutter-invenio-module/pull/98) was to
run doctest execution in pytest, removing the second `sphinx-build` invocation.

This both solved Travis CI build failures and simplified test suite execution.

Note that this change may necessitate to amend the code tests etc so that things
would be executed with the Flask application context (see
https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-marc21/pull/49/commits/09e98fc18fd5591dc65d806286e8da51b7e384b4).

What's the reasoning for keeping the angular templates in NPM? E.g.
```
<invenio-search-facets
template="{{ url_for('static', filename='node_modules/invenio-search-js/dist/templates/facets.html') }}">
</invenio-search-facets>
```

Is it as a kind of "separation of concerns"? Won't this make it really hard to customize the templates?

Could a solution be to at least have these paths defined in the config and thus being overridable?

issue_title: Create the files path based on the date of creation
issue_body:
Currently we are using the following path structure (invenio_files_rest.helpers.make_path):
```
base_uri/XX/YY/ZZZZ-ZZZZ-ZZZZ-ZZZZ-ZZZZZZZZZZZZ/data
```

Where `XXYYZZZZZZ-ZZZZ-ZZZZ-ZZZZ-ZZZZZZZZZZZZ` is the uuid of the file object. In order to help with the archival of the files, we should adapt it to a date based one, something like:
```
base_uri/YYYY/MM-wWW/ZZZZ-ZZZZ-ZZZZ-ZZZZ-ZZZZZZZZZZZZ/data
```

Where `YYYY` is the year, `MM` is the month, `WW` is the week number and `ZZZZ-ZZZZ-ZZZZ-ZZZZ-ZZZZZZZZZZZZ` the uuid of the file object.

When user is not found on the db, we automatically register them with `password=None`, as seen here: https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-oauthclient/blob/master/invenio_oauthclient/utils.py#L92-L103

But Passlib does not allow `None` passwords, although it allows empty ones (`''`). To verify it, run:
```
>>> from passlib.hash import oracle10
>>> hash = oracle10.encrypt(None, user='admin')
>>> hash = oracle10.encrypt('', user='admin')
```

Therefore, if the user is not already registered, an internal server error will occur.

Timezone-naive DateTime objects have the same .ISO() value but behave differently when localized to UTC:

```python

True

>>> unaware2.toZone('UTC').ISO()
'2016-06-15T05:00:00+00:00'

>>> unaware.toZone('UTC').ISO()
'2016-06-15T07:00:00+00:00'
```

issue_title: The SDL2 bootstrap can't make a Kivy Launcher
issue_body:
As in the title, the SDL2 bootstrap doesn't have any code to make a Kivy Launcher APK. It should be quite easy to add it just by looking at the way the pygame bootstrap does it, but the work needs doing.

The launcher could also be a separate bootstrap, but there might then be a lot of code duplication. Maybe there is another way to resolve this though.

My recipe/fabric/recipe.sh looks like below 
```
# version of your package
VERSION_fabric=${VERSION_fabric:-1.8.3}

# dependencies of this recipe
DEPS_fabric=(python setuptools)

# url of the package
URL_fabric=https://pypi.python.org/packages/source/F/Fabric/Fabric-1.8.3.tar.gz

# md5 of the package
MD5_fabric=02ce0ad4657f2d0ab6497a8fd3a09c2c

# default build path
BUILD_fabric=$BUILD_PATH/fabric/$(get_directory $URL_fabric)

# default recipe path
RECIPE_fabric=$RECIPES_PATH/fabric

# function called for preparing source code if needed
# (you can apply patch etc here.)
function prebuild_fabric() {
	true
}

# function called to build the source code
function build_fabric() {
    cd $BUILD_fabric
    export LDFLAGS="$LDFLAGS -L$LIBS_PATH"
    export LDSHARED="$LIBLINK"

    push_arm
    try $HOSTPYTHON setup.py install 
    pop_arm
}

# function called after all the compile have been done
function postbuild_fabric() {
	true
}
```


It fails with message like 
```
    import _io
ImportError: /home/kivy/.buildozer/android/platform/python-for-android/build/python-install/lib/python2.7/lib-dynload/_io.so: wrong ELF class: ELFCLASS32
```

Could you please me know whether recipe.sh has issues?

Do you have a document specifying the names of all the functions and operators in numexpr 3? (Including constants like `e` and `pi`: do you recognize these names or do they have to be in scope? In aggressive mode, which powers do you replace with explicit multiplication? Etc.)

I'm developing a histogramming package (https://github.com/diana-hep/histbook) that takes expressions as quantities to evaluate and plot (could address your #205). These expressions look a lot like numexpr and could benefit from being exactly the same language as numexpr (my [#22](https://github.com/diana-hep/histbook/issues/22) and [#23](https://github.com/diana-hep/histbook/issues/23). For instance, I should rename my "asin" to "arcsin" for complete compatibility.

But I see that you're in preparation for a big release, so I should probably aim for compatibility with the new version. Do you have a specification?

Thanks!

What's the problem? Who can help me?

The event dispatcher teardown has some issues:

            if event_name == "EventDispatcherShutdown":
                self._sl4a.closeSl4aSession()


This throws an error, but the error is squashed because it's done inside a Future whose result is never checked.

    self.poller.set_result("Done")

This is a private API for Executor implementations and should not be called externally.

     # The polling thread is guaranteed to finish after a max of 60 seconds,
     # so we don't wait here.
     self.executor.shutdown(wait=False)

Don't quite understand the comment here. Doesn't this mean the polling loop might still try to handle events after the server is torn down? And, couldn't this keep the python interpreter open for up to 60 seconds after the test ends?

        if self.sl4a:
            self.sl4a.closeSl4aSession()
            self.sl4a.close()
            self.sl4a = None
        if self.ed:
            self.ed.clean_up()
            self.ed = None

It's awkward and surprising that ed is torn down after its host sl4a session. Actually I'm surprised this works. Won't any sl4a commands clean_up tries to issue fail?

issue_title: Feature Request: call arbitrary function in a project by simply add fire in __main__.py
issue_body:
# Problem
If we want to call a function in a submodule of a project, we may need to replace existed __main__ function with fire.Fire(). every file which needs to add fire call need to do same things.

# Possible Implementation
We can just add a simple line fire.Fire('.') in __main__.py or other file. When we use following commands:
```bash
python -m project project.submodule1.add 1 2
python -m project .submodule1.add 1 2
python -m project submodule1.add 1 2
python main.py .submodule1.add 1 2
```
The fire object will find and import target file, analysis function/object signature and call it.

issue_title: Font does not show up on Apple Pages for Mac font selection pulldown on the sidebar
issue_body:
After installing the OTF version of FontAwesome on a Mac, it is not possible to select the font using the Format sidebar font selection pulldown in *Pages* because the font does not show up in the list at all.

If text already formatted using the FontAwesome font is insert into a *Pages* document and a cursor is placed on that text, the font pulldown shows an empty font name. Clicking on that pulldown makes the font "jump" to default to the first font sorted alphabetically.

The same problem does not happen on *Word for Mac*. The font shows up properly.

The same problem does not happen if using the OS font dialog box. The font shows up properly.

Although this may be a *Pages* problem, it is possible that some setting in the font is making it not show properly. Fixing this would make the font much more accessible to users using *Pages*.

The circular CC symbols, available here: http://creativecommons.org/about/downloads

They're available as images but having them as a web font would really increase the versatility, allowing for hover effects and the like.

### What can it be used for?
As you have mars-double and venus-double, which can be used to represent and male and female couple respectively, it would make sense to also have a transgender-double to represent a trans couple. 
Additionally, similar to the venus-mars pairing, trans-venus and trans-mars icons would also make sense.

### Example image (optional)
I think it's self explanatory

### Icon request checklist

- [x] This is a single icon or matched pair (Ex: `lock` / `unlock`)
- [x] The title starts with "Icon request: " and is followed by the requested icon name (Ex: `Icon request: magical-unicorn`)
- [x] I have [searched for existing issues](https://github.com/FortAwesome/Font-Awesome/issues) and to the best of my knowledge this is not a duplicate
- [x] I have included at least one example image if it could be helpful (optional)
- [x] The request is for a concrete object (it's harder to make an icon to represent happiness, it's easier to make a smiley face. ☺)

issue_title: [Question]How to handle a slave which has been disconnected from master for long time?
issue_body:
Let's say if I am using asynchronous streaming application with the below configuration in a 3 node cluster.

```
bootstrap:
  # this section will be written into Etcd:/<namespace>/<scope>/config after initializing new cluster
  # and all other cluster members will use it as a `global configuration`
  dcs:
    ttl: 30
    loop_wait: 10
    retry_timeout: 10
    maximum_lag_on_failover: 1048576
    postgresql:
      use_pg_rewind: true
      use_slots: true
      parameters:
        archive_mode: "off"
        wal_level: hot_standby
        max_wal_senders: 10
        wal_keep_segments: 100
        max_replication_slots: 10
        hot_standby: "on"
        wal_log_hints: "on"
        unix_socket_directories: '/tmp'
        max_connections: 400
        shared_buffers: 250MB
        autovacuum_analyze_scale_factor: 0.05
        autovacuum_vacuum_scale_factor: 0.10
        log_autovacuum_min_duration: 0
        autovacuum_naptime: 15s
        autovacuum_max_workers: 6
```

Let's say one of the slave node suddenly lose its connection to `master` for long time. 

1. In this case I think the size of XLOG on master will keep on building as the the XLOG are not being consumed by the disconnected slave's replication slot. So is there any setting in patorni configuration which will remove the slave and remove its replication slot if it is disconnected from master for x time duration? 
2. What is the recommended way to handle this case?

issue_title: HierarchicalRequirement object gives attribute error for Parent, HasParent is True
issue_body:
I retrieve a list of stories using get(...). Fetch is set to a list of fields, not to True. The list of fields contains both 'HasParent' and 'Parent'  I get through many stories in the list and the Parent attribute is always available when HasParent is true. Then I hit a story giving AttributeError on Parent when HasParent is true. If I get that story on its own (using FormattedID = "..."') in a query with fetch=True, the Parent attribute is present as expected.
Where should I start to look and what debugging should I turn on so I can give you a real issue report you stand a chance of fixing?

Guake 0.8.4
Ubuntu 14.04,16.04 x64

After running Midnight Commander over time dosen't work pasting text in Guake.

Copy "text"
Inserted "0~text1~"

Follow up #1236

You might actually want to use the [standard names](https://www.gnu.org/prep/standards/html_node/Directory-Variables.html) (i.e. lowercase `prefix`, `*dir`), something like this:

```Makefile
# Common prefix for installation directories.
# NOTE: This directory must exist when you start the install.
prefix = /usr/local
datarootdir = $(prefix)/share
datadir = $(datarootdir)
exec_prefix = $(prefix)
bindir = $(exec_prefix)/bin
localedir = $(datarootdir)/locale
gsettingsschemadir=$(datarootdir)/share/glib-2.0/schemas
…
```

I also think `INSTALL_ROOT` is commonly called [`DESTDIR`](https://www.gnu.org/prep/standards/html_node/DESTDIR.html).

issue_title: Fix EnumArray dtype type (should be np.dtype)
issue_body:
Hi there!

I really enjoy OpenFisca, but I recently encountered an issue.

### Here is what I did:
````
    month = '2005-01'
    simulation = new_simulation(tax_benefit_system, month)
    var = simulation.calculate('housing_occupancy_status', month)
    print(var.dtype.kind)
````

### Here is what I expected to happen:

No error

### Here is what actually happened:

AttributeError: type object 'numpy.int16' has no attribute 'kind'

## Context

Bug appeared when using [`create_data_frame_by_entity`](https://github.com/openfisca/openfisca-survey-manager/blob/master/openfisca_survey_manager/scenarios.py#L302) with Enum variables.

It is linked to a wrong use of dtype in [EnumArray](https://github.com/openfisca/openfisca-core/blob/e97a3ed33bc3980a8bc27fbe81d17bdeeff9f999/openfisca_core/indexed_enums.py#L59)
wich is numpy type and not of class numpy.dtype, as you can see in the following example:
 ````
print(type(np.dtype(np.int16)))
print(np.dtype(np.int16).kind)
print(type(np.int16))
print(np.int16.kind)
````

I identify more as a:

- Analyst _(I make macroscopic computations on real populations)_.
- Developer _(I create tools that use the existing OpenFisca code)_.

issue_title: Provide a "Porting from Paster" wiki page
issue_body:
```
Some notes from the porting - it could perhaps be the missing(?) documentation
for migrating from paster to gearbox:

Replace paster
    summary = 'yada yada'
with the first line of the docstring of the Command class ... or override
get_description.

Note: All newlines in the docstring will be collapsed and mangle the long help
text.

Grouping of commands is not possible. Standard commands (for development) can't
be customized under the same name or hidden. (Like for paster, the conceptual
model also assumes that the sub-command naming is namespaced so commands from
other packages won't conflict.)

The usage help is fully automated from the declared options.

For all deprecated Commands, replace paster
    hidden = True
with gearbox
    deprecated = True

Note: config_file, takes_config_file, min_args and max_args are not available /
relevant.

The gearbox parser is customized by overriding get_parser - there is nothing
like paster update_parser.

Gearbox is using argparse instead of optparse ... but argparse add_argument is
mostly backwards compatible with optparse add_option.

Instead of overriding command or run as in paster, override take_action in
gearbox. The parsed arguments are passed to take_action, not available on the
command instance.
```

datadog tracing (https://github.com/DataDog/dd-trace-py) has the ability to disable tracing via a `enabled` parameter to their `configure` method (https://github.com/DataDog/dd-trace-py/blob/master/ddtrace/tracer.py#L82).  This is really useful so one can control the behavior of tracing via an environment variable for example without having to change code, allowing for local and prod behaviors.  I suggest a similar mechanism for xray

issue_title: Attention Example is Not Efficient, Needs Greedy Decoding
issue_body:
Currently the attention example is not very efficient, particularly on GPUs. For example, this for loop could be changed so it only does a single matrix multiplication (which can be done only once per sentence):
https://github.com/clab/dynet/blob/master/examples/python/attention.py#L75

Also, here the attention model is randomly generating examples instead of selecting the best one, which is more in line with what we would expect:
https://github.com/clab/dynet/blob/master/examples/python/attention.py#L105

I'm installing dynet with python interface (GPU supported), and followed the instruction at manual installation section. I successfully installed dynet at my home dir. However when I import dynet, it complains the following error:
`ImportError: libdynet.so: cannot open shared object file: No such file or directory`
I checked the location of _libdynet.so_ which is at $PATH_TO_DYNET/build_gpu/dynet/, so I did this suggestion `export DYLD_LIBRARY_PATH=$PATH_TO_DYNET/build_gpu/dynet/:$DYLD_LIBRARY_PATH`, but still report this error.
I can import dynet under $PATH_TO_DYNET/build_gpu/python without any trouble. Can someone help me fix this issue.

**Environment:
Linux distribution: Linux version 2.6.32-431.5.1.el6.centos.plus.x86_64
Python: Python 2.7.11**

issue_title: conv2d causes out-of-memory error in beam search
issue_body:
My beam search uses conv2d for tagging a long image sequence with beam size 10. It seems that conv2d accumulate the memory usage until the memory is used up. The error is as below:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/16813308/27738489-0af16ad8-5dde-11e7-9e79-e3fb3820ab8b.png)

Can you provide a method for using conv2d in beam search mode? The out-of-memory error even with a small beam size will prevent the convolution computation in dynet.

issue_title: Error when using composites of parameter and batch

issue_body:
Hi there!

I have been experiencing some issues using dropout. The thing is that it makes my system many times slower to train. Before dropout it took ~6h in GPU to run a full epoch. Now, it takes ~24h. That's a 4 fold increase. The only change in my code was do use dropout in the hidden layers of the net. Also, GPU usage is now way down, and a lot of time it actually reaches 0, which didn't happen before.

Any clues?

issue_title: v0.6.0 seems to break oddly for LinkedIn
issue_body:
When using v0.5.0 and fetching an Access Token from LinkedIn this works as expected. When we upgrade to v0.6.0 however, the fetching succeeds but the Access Token starts out as invalid. It can take up to 4 minutes before the LinkedIn API accepts the Access Token as valid. During that time an exception like this gets thrown:

```
Exception: {
  "errorCode": 0,
  "message": "Unable to verify access token",
  "requestId": "L595QANTTL",
  "status": 401,
  "timestamp": 1454600867781
}
```

Like said, after up to 4 minutes that very same Access Token starts to work. Of course this is a problem as you can't really login a user in your own implementation that way. Please note that this seems to only take place when you are logged _out_ of www.linkedin.com - so when the OAuth Dialog first asks you to log in to your LinkedIn account. When you are already logged in and it just flies through the OAuth2 dialog it works (most of the time).

We've diffed version v0.5.0 against v0.6.0 and what seems to be causing it is a line in oauth2_session.py at line number 200:

```
auth = auth or requests.auth.HTTPBasicAuth(username, password)
```

When I comment out that line, all starts to work correctly for LinkedIn, so it seems that it's adding BasicAuth headers with empty username and password and that in turn seems to get LinkedIn upset, where it refuses to allow immediate access to the API even though they provided an actual Access Token.

issue_body:
https://travis-ci.org/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/builds/159670988

PS @tseaver The expected failures was 50-50 in that build.

This query works fine...

SELECT page.* FROM `zentinel-datawarehouse.xxx.ga_sessions_20170601` ga,
UNNEST (hits) hits,
UNNEST (hits.page) page

but when i need use _table_suffix
SELECT page.* FROM `zentinel-datawarehouse.xxx.ga_sessions_*` ga,
UNNEST (hits) hits,
UNNEST (hits.page) page
WHERE _TABLE_SUFFIX>=20170601

Dont works any more...
This happened in thats date because hits.page is record repeteabled... in month 08 or 09 works fine because is record NULL
ANY IDEA???

REGARDS

The polling library in google-cloud-core contains an indirect native dependency (`ctypes`) via the `tenacity` library. This has the unfortunate consequence of causing import failures on App Engine because the GAE runtime restricts the use of native dependencies.

To clarify the dependency chain causing the problem:

| Project | Description | Relevant Import Site
|--------|------------|-------------
|`google-cloud-core`||[`core/google/api/core/future/polling.py`](https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/blob/0723a9cb802a08c828af83049c80b68036fcfc3c/core/google/api/core/future/polling.py#L23)
|[`tenacity`](https://github.com/jd/tenacity)|General-purpose retry library|[`monotonic` import](https://github.com/jd/tenacity/blob/319168f18ecb7d95ead91f3d7aaada23dabe1161/tenacity/__init__.py#L28)
|[monotonic](https://github.com/atdt/monotonic)|py27 backport of py33 `time.monotonic`|[`ctypes` import](https://github.com/atdt/monotonic/blob/5c0322dc559bf961f7e111d97cd3ed9ab5c1a73b/monotonic.py#L41)|

I couldn't think of a particularly easy fix.

One wacky idea that might work would be to write a monotonic-like function/class that faked monotonicity by storing some fixed number of recent calls to the non-monotonic function (e.g. `time.time()`). When a new call came in, it would call the time function and instead of returning that value straight away, it would calculate and return the max of the value and the cached previous calls (and, to be definitionally correct, add an epsilon). Given the constraints of tenacity, this might suffice but a simpler solution would obviously be preferred.

Thanks :)

issue_title: can't pickle using dill for pyutilib.workflow objects
issue_body:
I'm trying to pickle (using the dill extension) a workflow object from pyutilib.workflow like so using python 2.7. The end objective here is to be able to insert these workflow objects into a MongoDB database and pulled out on the other end when needed:

    from pyutilib import workflow
    import testworkflow
    from bson.binary import Binary
    import pickle
    import dill
    import weakref

    A = testworkflow.testTask()
    w = workflow.Workflow()
    w.add(A)

    with open('w.dill', 'wb') as f:
        scriptbytes = dill.dump(w, f)
    script.close()

testworkflow.py only contains testTask(), which is written as follows:

    import pyutilib.workflow

    class testTask(pyutilib.workflow.Task):

    def __init__(self, *args, **kwds):
        pyutilib.workflow.Task.__init__(self, *args, **kwds)
        self.inputs.declare('x')
        self.inputs.declare('y')
        self.outputs.declare('z')

    def execute(self):
        self.z = self.x + self.y
But when I attempt to execute it to serialize the workflow object, I get a massive traceback list from the pickle.py file, at the very bottom of which is simply "AssertionError".

It seems to have troubles with things like:

  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/pickle.py", line 649, in save_dict
    self._batch_setitems(obj.iteritems())
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/pickle.py", line 681, in _batch_setitems
    save(v)
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/pickle.py", line 286, in save
    f(self, obj) # Call unbound method with explicit self
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/dill/dill.py", line 905, in save_weakref
    pickler.save_reduce(_create_weakref, (refobj,), obj=obj)
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/pickle.py", line 405, in save_reduce
    self.memoize(obj)
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/pickle.py", line 244, in memoize
    assert id(obj) not in self.memo

The above chunk of traceback is, seriously, about 1% of the total list. Several tracebacks are to the same line of code, so is it a circular reference problem? I'm absolutely new to this type of project and I've searched all over for other related questions but none really seem to be quite relevant enough.

Am I missing some newer libraries? Is there a better way to do this?

EDIT: as per Martijn Pieters' helpful comment

> Pickling is a recursive process, which is why you see certain lines repeated. The process ends up back at an object that was pickled before (id(obj) in self.memo is true only if the object was already processed).

So how can I stop this condition from being triggered? Why can't pickling automatically ignore already-serialized chunks as a base case in recursion?

issue_title: [engine] generate concrete subgraphs of nodes using the rule graph
issue_body: With the rule graph, we can generate subgraphs until we need to switch subjects. Instead of generating dependencies one set at a time, generate and insert nodes into the graph until reaching an edge to a rule graph entry that can't be turned into a node yet.

Currently these components are coordinated by `Task` awkwardly. It exhaustively deals with cache reads in `invalidated` but hands off writes to the `InvalidationCacheManager` via an opaque callback. In general it would be nice to present a single cache api to Task to use. The BuildInvalidator would live behind that api and could then more easily be gotten rid of down the line.

This work was brought to light by changes and discussion in #4686

issue_title: leverage pytest's `--confcutdir` option to isolate conftest.py loading within the buildroot
issue_body:
A user reported an issue where a `conftest.py` living in his home directory was breaking python tests when run via pants.

After a bit of investigation, it appears that pytest apparently has some odd behavior wrt conftest.py discovery. The current algorithm will walk upwards in directories from your test until it locates a `pytest.ini`, `tox.ini` or `setup.cfg` file - deeming that directory the rootdir. Within the rootdir, all `conftest.py` files are fair game. Furthermore, paths which have `conftest.py` files appear to be automatically added to sys.path (see: https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/issues/82).

This means if a user has a directory layout with a buildroot like `/Users/kwlzn/dev/monorepo`, pytest will walk all paths upwards into the root dir - including the users home directory. If none of the sentinel files are found, all `conftest.py` files in dirs upward to the root are fair game. This seems somewhat undesirable from a `repeatable builds` perspective.

pytest has an option called `--confcutdir`:

```
   confcutdir

   Sets a directory where search upwards for `conftest.py` files stops.
   By default, pytest will stop searching for `conftest.py` files upwards
   from `pytest.ini`/`tox.ini`/`setup.cfg` of the project if any,
   or up to the file-system root.
```

which we can use to lock this behavior into the base of the buildroot. this should hopefully improve the 'not on my machine'/repeatability mechanics.

issue_title: style is changed when replace text in table
A follow up on the issue of Figure caption
issue_body:
Thanks to the latest developments presented in #137 it is now possible to insert an automatic figure or table caption using the following code.

def Figure(paragraph):
    run = run = paragraph.add_run()
    r = run._r
    fldChar = OxmlElement('w:fldChar')
    fldChar.set(qn('w:fldCharType'), 'begin')
    r.append(fldChar)
    instrText = OxmlElement('w:instrText')
    instrText.text = ' SEQ Figure \* ARABIC'
    r.append(instrText)
    fldChar = OxmlElement('w:fldChar')
    fldChar.set(qn('w:fldCharType'), 'end')
    r.append(fldChar)


def Table(paragraph):
    run = run = paragraph.add_run()
    r = run._r
    fldChar = OxmlElement('w:fldChar')
    fldChar.set(qn('w:fldCharType'), 'begin')
    r.append(fldChar)
    instrText = OxmlElement('w:instrText')
    instrText.text = ' SEQ Table \* ARABIC'
    r.append(instrText)
    fldChar = OxmlElement('w:fldChar')
    fldChar.set(qn('w:fldCharType'), 'end')
    r.append(fldChar)

issue_body:
Its seems that AWS Lambda only allows writes to `/tmp`. When imageio tries to download ffmpeg, it first tries `~`, then `/var/tmp` and finally fails because neither are writeable ([relevant code](https://github.com/imageio/imageio/blob/master/imageio/core/util.py#L441) -- also see https://github.com/imageio/imageio/issues/54).

Instead of trying yet another directory (`/tmp`), it would be handy if we were able to configure this using an environment variable, say `IMAGEIO_USERDIR`.

issue_title: When using the --timestamp option, the names of files in the 'scout2-report/inc-awsconfig/' directory contain the timestamp twice.
issue_body:
When using the `--timestamp` option, the names of files in the `scout2-report/inc-awsconfig/` directory contain the timestamp twice.

For example, the HTML source may be trying to load the following JavaScript files:

```html
<script src="inc-awsconfig/aws_config-xxxxxxxx-2017-04-24_11h58-0600.js"></script>
<script src="inc-awsconfig/exceptions-xxxxxxxx-2017-04-24_11h58-0600.js"></script>
```

But the actual files generated by Scout2 have the following filenames:

```
inc-awsconfig/aws_config-xxxxxxxx-2017-04-24_11h58-0600-2017-04-24_11h58-0600.js
inc-awsconfig/exceptions-xxxxxxxx-2017-04-24_11h58-0600-2017-04-24_11h58-0600.js
```

Please follow the [semantic versioning](http://semver.org/) standard when tagging releases. At the very least, this would mean:

1. Always have three numbers in the version e.g. 0.9.0
2. The successor to 0.9.0 should be 0.10.0 not 0.99.0

This would make it much easier for packages depending on html5lib-python to specify dependencies. I think version 1.0.0 is a great time to make the switch.
thanks

issue_title: Problem while decoding asterix data cat 021
issue_body:
Hi here
I want to decode asterix data cat 021 files and I got the following errors:

> DEBUG:   Mar 15 15:58:13 2018 (InputParser.cpp) [15 00 2B FF A1 DA B9 E0 00 28 A8 BF 93 F9 50 0D 20 91 14 40 07 EE 17 30 00 07 08 05 78 00 00 08 B7 5D 88 08 15 F2 C3 6E 60 00 00]
ERROR:   Mar 15 15:58:13 2018 (asterixformat.cxx) DataItem::parse needed length=2 , and there is only 0 : [  ]
ERROR:   Mar 15 15:58:13 2018 (asterixformat.cxx) Wrong length in DataItem format for CAT021/I145
ERROR:   Mar 15 15:58:13 2018 (asterixformat.cxx) Data Record bytes: [ FF A1 DA B9 E0 00 28 A8 BF 93 F9 50 0D 20 91 14 40 07 EE 17 30 00 07 08 05 78 00 00 08 B7 5D 88 08 15 F2 C3 6E 60 00 00 ]
ERROR:   Mar 15 15:58:13 2018 (asterixformat.cxx) Record not parsed properly. CAT021 len=40




Can anyone help me to identify the issue? is the problem from the files or from the application?

I have found a wrong parameter in asterix_cat010_1_.xml

The data item 41 (Position in WGS-84 Coordinates) has a scale factor for both latitude and longitude of 0.000005364418029785156.

The Eurocontrol Specifications say that this scale must be 180/2^31. So, the scale factor must be 0,00000008381903171539306640625.

I made some tests with asterix cat 010 with a Southampton port radar and with this new value, the software seems to work fine and the final coordinates are correct.


Thanks a lot
Nacho

issue_title: Throw API specific Exceptions instead of the Exception class
issue_body:
When errors happen inside the API, always Exceptions from type Exception are thrown.
This makes it impossible to:
- determine if an exception came from the API or other code in the script
- react depending on the type of error

Instead create an own Exception class deriving from Exception and throw this one. Even better would be to use different type of exceptions depending on the error, e.g:
PBNotAuthorizedError
PBRessourceDoesNotExist

issue_title: Have to pass dictionary of kwargs as positional arg to create_endpoint_config
issue_body:
**Issue**
Contrary to documentation, create_endpoint_config does not take keyword arguments, it takes self, args, and kwargs as positional arguments, which requires a call with only kwargs to use None for the args position. This is confusing and should at least be documented properly, but it would be better if the desired behavior listed in the documentation were the true behavior of the code.

**Details**
When I try to use create_endpoint_config to provide an ipv4 address to my container, I get
```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages/docker/api/container.py", line 148, in create_endpoint_config
    return create_endpoint_config(self._version, *args, **kwargs)
TypeError: create_endpoint_config() got an unexpected keyword argument 'ipv4_address'
```

I'm using the following to create my network config:
```
new_client = Client(base_url="unix:///var/run/docker.sock")
new_client.start(new_client.create_container(image="nginx",name="test",detach=True,
             networking_config=new_client.create_networking_config({
                  'docker_macvlan':new_client.create_endpoint_config(ipv4_address='10.10.10.14')
             })
))
```

I tried:

- `new_client.create_endpoint_config(ipv4_address='10.10.10.14')`
  - Error as above
- `new_client.create_endpoint_config(kwargs={'ipv4_address':'10.10.10.14'})`
  - Similar error as above: unexpected keyword kwargs
- `new_client.create_endpoint_config({'ipv4_address':'10.10.10.14'})`
  - JSON parsing error: `docker.errors.APIError: 500 Server Error: Internal Server Error ("json: cannot unmarshal string into Go value of type []string")`
- `new_client.create_endpoint_config('10.10.10.14')`
  - The JSON parsing error again

issue_title: isalive(): check process status for non-child process
issue_body:
The original implementation of isalive uses os.waitpid to check if the ptyprocess is alive
os.waitpid can only check the process status if that process is a child of the current process   

If the pexpect object is passed from process A to process B
process B can access the pexpect object but it cannot use it because os.waitpid can't find the ptyprocess under the child list of process B
it will throw the no such process error
while the process actually exists and is still running   

To solve this
It can use os.kill to send signal 0 to check if process is alive
which achieve the same functionality of os.waitpid but not limit it to child process   

Is it possible to add this as an extra check for isalive()?

issue_title: All exceptions coming from the driver should share a base class

We have a few exception classes:

* `Error`
* `UnsupportedConfigurationError`

The number may still grow. They should all share a common base class so clients can `except` any error coming from the module.

We have a number of APIs that service an AngularJS application, one of these APIs is legacy and will be replaced in time but for now we proxy requests from the newer API to the legacy one (HapiJS to nginx/PHP/FastCGI). This works perfectly on the Deis platform in both SSL and non-SSL.

However, when we turn on `enforceHTTPS` to make sure that all requests are in HTTPS our legacy API endpoint is exposed to the AngularJS application and we get a CORS error. The CORS error is expected as the legacy API should never have requests made to it directly from the application.

What I believe is happening is that with `enforceHTTPS` set to true on the router the request from the new API to the legacy API is being redirected which in turn exposes the legacy API endpoint. As the redirect only happens with `enforceHTTPS` set to true I think the block of code in the nginx template below is doing the redirect...

https://github.com/deis/deis/blob/v1.9.0/router/image/templates/nginx.conf#L303

We are running v1.9.0 of the Deis platform on AWS using a Cephless install. SSL termination is done on the Deis router as later on we require the use of sockets which AWS ELBs do not support.

All of out API endpoints only ever make HTTPS calls so there should never be a cause for the Deis router to action a redirect.

issue_title: Make store component optional by allowing external database, registry, and log collector
issue_body:
Related to #2252 and #2295.

It seems to me like the store and Ceph add a lot of complexity to the Deis cluster. The store component is obviously useful, maybe necessary for applications that require everything to be self-contained in the cluster. However, for someone like me with little Devops experience, it would be nice to have the option to offload some of this complexity to other services that require little or no management on my part.

For example, from an AWS perspective I could ideally replace the database component with RDS, remove the logger component and have the logspouts post logs to a service like Loggly or Papertrail, and have the the registry use S3 as storage. If I'm correct, then no components would rely on the store and deis-store-daemon, deis-store-gateway, deis-store-metdata, and deis-store-monitor could all be removed from the cluster.

Some benefits that I could see from my perspective:

1. No need for Ceph experience to debug issues with the store. (I took down a node in the cluster without fully removing it from Ceph, and managed to leave my cluster in an unrecoverable state.)
2. Nodes are stateless - which make it much easier to scale down the cluster. No need to go through the process of tearing-down Ceph - just shut down the server.
3. Core components use less of the cluster's resources.

This could potentially make upgrading the Deis platform easier as well, but I'm not sure.

I just wanted to throw out this idea to see what the potential downsides are for this strategy, and what the Deis team's opinion is on supporting something like this.

issue_title: AWS autoscaler terminated then restarted node - how to get cluster back to normal?
issue_body:
For some reason which I'm still trying to figure out, my AWS autoscaling group thought one of my nodes in my 3-node cluster was unhealthy, terminated it, then created a new node in its place. It's pretty vague on the reasoning behind terminating it. All it says is: `At 2015-01-17T21:35:37Z an instance was taken out of service in response to a EC2 instance status checks failure.`

As a result of the automated termination and restart, my ceph cluster and deis-store components seem to be in an unhealthy state. Also, it seems to have crashed one of my applications. The `deis-store-volume` container on the new host is stuck in `start-pre` mode on one of the machines in the cluster, but everything else looks fine according to `fleetctl list-units`.

```
deis-store-daemon.service	3e465ae1.../10.21.1.1	active		running

deis-store-volume.service	812989d1.../10.21.1.3	activating	start-pre
```

However, when I check the status with `deisctl status deis-store-<component>` I get the message: `Unit deis-store-volume.service does not appear to be running.` for every store component except `deis-store-gateway` which appears healthy.

I tried getting the status of the ceph cluster with `ceph -s`, but it never returns the status, just shows ping-link ouput, then eventually times-out.

I stopped and unloaded the crashed application via `fleetctl`, but when I try to scale it back up with `deis ps:scale cmd=1` it just hangs. I'm assuming this is because of the deis store being corrupt. 

Another odd thing I thought I'd note - When running `df -h` on the two original nodes in the cluster, the command just hangs, while `df -l`. Both commands work fine on the newly spawned node.

I'm using Deis v1.2.0.

First, what are the steps I need to take to get my cluster back into a healthy state? Second, is there anyway to find out more information about why my autoscaler thought that the node was healthy? How can I prevent this from happening in the future?

issue_title: deisctl should warn when unit files have errors
issue_body:
We're using tags to isolate the control plane and application units to different hosts/autoscaling groups in EC2.

We ran into an issue earlier this week where a node failed and some of the deis control plane units moved to an unexpected node because the MachineMetadata option was incorrectly specified in some of the deis unit files ( MachineMetaData instead of MachineMetadata ).

Fleetctl will load the unit, but will give you a warning. It would be nice if deisctl did something similar or perhaps rejected the submission outright.

$ grep MachineMetaData deis-controller.service
MachineMetaData=role=services

$ fleetctl --version
fleetctl version 0.11.1

$ fleetctl submit deis-controller
2015/07/17 08:22:36 WARN fleetctl.go:576: Unit deis-controller.service: unrecognized requirement in [X-Fleet] section: "MachineMetaData"

I've already resolved this issue. But I wrote this for sharing.
As it will be encountered almost all people who deploy Deis on Azure. 
The behavior of this issue is similar to #2330. But it seems something wrong.


I deployed 3 nodes into Azure using scripts provided by Azure.
And I executed `deisctl platform start`. But stuck at deis-store-monitor.

issue_title: deis-router error_log should have etcd configurable error level
issue_body:
When debugging issues with `deis-router` it might be difficult to track the error down since we are relying on the default logging level in `error_log` - `error`. While there are log levels going all the way down to `debug`. [ngx_core_module#error_log](http://nginx.org/en/docs/ngx_core_module.html#error_log)

We already compile with `--with-debug`. So all we need to do is expose the ability to change `error_log` via a `etcd` key.

(This should be a quick 2 line fix. One in nginx.conf and one in docs.)

issue_title: Safe string conversions can be evaded by objects with `__str__` returning bytestrings
issue_body:
The safe string conversion we added for the `TransferClient` to handle UUID endpoint IDs is not bulletproof.

If it sees a bytestring directly, it will re-encode it as a string type.
However, if it sees an unknown type and relies on `__str__` to produce a string type, it may be disappointed.
Well-behaved types should have `__str__` returning a bytestring or raw string in py2 and returning unicode in py3, but apparently not even `uuid.UUID` meets that definition of "well-behaved".

If this turns out to be a problem in the future, we may have to more strictly sanitize things that we'd really like to be strings or unicodes by capturing the results of `__str__` and trying our song-and-dance over again.

issue_title: Friendlier, action-oriented notifications for update availability
issue_body:
Maybe we can provide a hint on how to update:

Today:
`A new version of PyGraphistry is available (installed=0.9.19 latest=0.9.22).`

Proposal:

"A new version of PyGraphistry is available (installed=0.9.19 latest=0.9.22). Try running `pip install "graphistry" --upgrade` or `pip install "graphistry[all]]" --upgrade`."

Once #570 is merged we should have all components in place to start a very simple test network.
This is a proposal for an extremely basic one with the goal to implement it as quickly as possible:

The network is solitary and responsible for a single shard only. The topology is uniform, i.e.
nodes connect randomly to other nodes in the network. All nodes try to download all collations.
Anyone can create collations and does so at random intervals unless they have seen a collation for
the current period already. Newly created collations are broadcasted. The protocol is based on devp2p and supports the following three message types:

- ~`NewCollationHashes`: Announces hashes of collations that are newly available at the sending node
  and are not known to be already available at the receiving node.~
- ~`GetCollations`: Request a set of collations.~
- `Collations`: ~Response to a `GetCollations` message, containing 0 or more of the requested
  collations.~ Broadcast collations to a peer.

## Rationale

devp2p is used as there's no libp2p implementation in Python yet.

It's a single network to keep things simple and require a smaller number of nodes. The goal for now
is to test distribution of collations, the next iteration could be multiple networks to test notary
switching.

Anyone can propose collations to avoid dependency on the SMC and the SMC handler. For the same reason, a period should be defined as some absolute time interval instead of a main chain block interval.

issue_title: LightPeerChain should serve chain data as well
issue_body:
### What is wrong?

Currently, our `LightPeerChain` implementation only has APIs for retrieving chain data from other peers, but it simply drops any requests for chain data from peers

### How can it be fixed

It should handle those requests for chain data. That will require our `LESPeer` implementation to grow a few APIs, some of which already exist in `tests/p2p/test_lightchain.py`

      File "/home/joris/miniconda3/lib/python3.5/site-packages/conda/egg_info.py", line 86, in get_egg_info

        for fn in os.listdir(sp_dir):
    FileNotFoundError: [Errno 2] No such file or directory: '/home/joris/scipy/dateutil'
```

The reason for this is that I once did `pip install -e .` in the  '/home/joris/scipy/dateutil' directory to test out a dev install of dateutil. But later I removed it. Until now it was not a problem, but so now it is starting to give problems with conda 4.4. 
I know I can fix this by removing it manually from easy-install.pth, but IMO it could be handles more gracefully by conda.

issue_title: conda install new env picks up pip installs
issue_body:
There seems to be a problem with conda install, it seems to pick up my pip install in one environment for a new environment.

1. `conda create -n debug python=3`
2. `conda install --yes conda install --yes pandas pytables matplotlib basemap scipy numpy scikit-learn scikit-image gdal bokeh dask ipython notebook ipywidgets ipyparallel astropy cython pyzmq ipykernel jupyter pip nomkl`

The suggested packages are this (removed irrelevant):
```bash
nbformat:         4.0.1-py35_0
    networkx:         1.11-py35_0
    nomkl:            1.0-0
    notebook:         4.1.0-py35_2
    numexpr:          2.5-np110py35_nomkl_0    [nomkl]

and when done I do a `conda list` and suddenly a dev version of notebook appears:

which is also active in the new environment:
```bash

Correct jupyter to .jupyter ? [nyae] n
5.0.0.dev
```

I'm on OSX, using newest conda:

issue_title: Cannot reinstall packages with conda - Anaconda 2
issue_body:
## Current Behavior I am using Anaconda2 and Python 2.7. I reinstalled it and I am adding packages through conda. In particular, I tried installing the R Kernel but it didn't work. Last week I did the same operation and it was working -- and the machine did not have changes in its configuration. I encounter the same problem with other packages. The error I get is an EncodingError. I tried running ```$ conda create -n py2 python=2 ``` and ```conda activate py2```but it did not help. I would like to keep on using Python 2.7 (because it is required for other reasons). Any help would be very much appreciated. ### Steps to Reproduce * Install Anaconda2 (https://repo.anaconda.com/archive/Anaconda2-5.2.0-Linux-x86_64.sh) * Ensure that I have ```/home/user/username/anaconda2/bin:``` in PATH * Execute ```conda install -c r r-irkernel ``` * I get as response: ``` EncodingError: A unicode encoding or decoding error has occurred. Python 2 is the interpreter under which conda is running in your base environment. Replacing your base environment with one having Python 3 may help resolve this issue. If you still have a need for Python 2 environments, consider using 'conda create' and 'conda activate'. For example: $ conda create -n py2 python=2 $ conda activate py2 Error details: UnicodeEncodeError('ascii', u'r-dbi\xa0', 5, 6, 'ordinal not in range(128)') ``` * I tried installing ```conda install -c r r-essentials ```and it worked, but then I get the same error with some other packages like ``` conda install -c r r-dbi ```, while ```conda install -c r r-rmariadb``` worked. ## Expected Behavior * The R kernel should be installed * A new notebook should be possible to be created using R ## Environment Information

issue_title: allow_other_channels False in the system .condarc doesn't stop install
issue_body:
conda version 4.2.13
I added allow_other_channels: False to my system .condarc then I added a new channel in my ~/.condarc file and I could successfully install a package from the channel in my ~/.condarc.

The channel in my ~/.condarc is also used when doing conda search but it probably shouldn't be.

The feature to control the channels from the system .condarc file is a critical feature for enterprises to control conda use.

Please fix this ASAP!

issue_title: conda downloads plain repodata.json instead of the .bz2 from conda.anaconda.org
issue_body:
**I'm submitting a...** - [x] bug report (more or less) - [ ] feature request Noticed that in https://github.com/conda/conda/issues/7239#issuecomment-387528831. ## Current Behavior `conda` downloads `repodata.json` instead of their `repodata.json.bz2` versions from `conda.anaconda.org`. ### Steps to Reproduce
``` conda --version conda clean -yi time conda create --dry-run -vvnx 2>&1 | grep 'make_request' time conda create --dry-run -vvnx -c conda-forge 2>&1 | grep 'make_request' ``` ``` conda 4.5.2 DEBUG urllib3.connectionpool:_make_request(396): https://repo.anaconda.com:443 "GET /pkgs/pro/noarch/repodata.json.bz2 HTTP/1.1" 200 110 DEBUG urllib3.connectionpool:_make_request(396): https://repo.anaconda.com:443 "GET /pkgs/r/noarch/repodata.json.bz2 HTTP/1.1" 200 372 DEBUG urllib3.connectionpool:_make_request(396): https://repo.anaconda.com:443 "GET /pkgs/free/noarch/repodata.json.bz2 HTTP/1.1" 200 1928 DEBUG urllib3.connectionpool:_make_request(396): https://repo.anaconda.com:443 "GET /pkgs/main/noarch/repodata.json.bz2 HTTP/1.1" 200 22384 DEBUG urllib3.connectionpool:_make_request(396): https://repo.anaconda.com:443 "GET /pkgs/main/linux-64/repodata.json.bz2 HTTP/1.1" 200 392536 DEBUG urllib3.connectionpool:_make_request(396): https://repo.anaconda.com:443 "GET /pkgs/pro/linux-64/repodata.json.bz2 HTTP/1.1" 200 26668 DEBUG urllib3.connectionpool:_make_request(396): https://repo.anaconda.com:443 "GET /pkgs/r/linux-64/repodata.json.bz2 HTTP/1.1" 200 122248 DEBUG urllib3.connectionpool:_make_request(396): https://repo.anaconda.com:443 "GET /pkgs/free/linux-64/repodata.json.bz2 HTTP/1.1" 200 437385 real 0m4.168s user 0m3.559s sys 0m0.138s DEBUG urllib3.connectionpool:_make_request(396): https://conda.anaconda.org:443 "GET /conda-forge/noarch/repodata.json HTTP/1.1" 200 None DEBUG urllib3.connectionpool:_make_request(396): https://conda.anaconda.org:443 "GET /conda-forge/linux-64/repodata.json HTTP/1.1" 200 None real 0m8.288s user 0m6.808s sys 0m0.207s ``` ## Expected Behavior Depending on internet connection / CPU speed ratio, downloading `repodata.json.bz2` could be faster. ``` $ time bash -c 'curl -sL https://conda.anaconda.org/conda-forge/linux-64/repodata.json | wc -c' 18622967 real 0m2.479s user 0m0.098s sys 0m0.161s $ time bash -c 'curl -sL https://conda.anaconda.org/conda-forge/linux-64/repodata.json.bz2 | bzip2 -d | wc -c' 18622967 real 0m1.336s user 0m0.522s sys 0m0.075s ``` It would make sense to generally try to download `repodata.json.bz2` first and use `repodata.json` as a fallback. Also, note that for packages from `repo.anaconda.com` the `repodata.json.bz2` files are downloaded. ## Environment Information
`conda info`

``` active environment : base active env location : /opt/conda shell level : 1 user config file : /root/.condarc populated config files : conda version : 4.5.2 conda-build version : not installed python version : 3.6.5.final.0 base environment : /opt/conda (writable) channel URLs : https://repo.anaconda.com/pkgs/main/linux-64 https://repo.anaconda.com/pkgs/main/noarch https://repo.anaconda.com/pkgs/free/linux-64 https://repo.anaconda.com/pkgs/free/noarch https://repo.anaconda.com/pkgs/r/linux-64 https://repo.anaconda.com/pkgs/r/noarch https://repo.anaconda.com/pkgs/pro/linux-64 https://repo.anaconda.com/pkgs/pro/noarch package cache : /opt/conda/pkgs /root/.conda/pkgs envs directories : /opt/conda/envs /root/.conda/envs platform : linux-64 user-agent : conda/4.5.2 requests/2.18.4 CPython/3.6.5 Linux/4.14.31-1-MANJARO debian/9 glibc/2.24 UID:GID : 0:0 netrc file : None offline mode : False ```

`conda config --show-sources`

Hi,

Is it possible to add support for merging multiple old indices in one.

In the case of having an index per day, after a couple of months, we are running into having a lot of openned indices and Elasticsearch becomes slow because of that.

It would be nice if we were able to merge the old indices by weeks, by months or by year.

By example, instead of having 60 openned indices (one per day) for the last 2 months, merging by weeks the indices older than 7 days would results in:
```
  7 openned indices for the last 7 days
+ 7 openned indices for the other 7 weeks
-----
  14 openned indices (instead of 60 indices)
```
This would really help for the memory management of Elasticsearch and will help for sure the response time while querying in Kibana.

Thanks,

David

issue_title: '+' character in document id replaced with space character in low-level client index operation
issue_body:
Indexing a document into elasticsearch using the low-level client as such:
```
es_client = elasticsearch.Elasticsearch([ES_URL])
es_client.index(
    index=index, doc_type=doc_type,
    id='2017-04-14T16:04:45.000000+0000',
    body=body
)
```
results in an indexed document where `_id="2017-04-14T16:04:45.000000 0000"`. The `+` character is being replaced with a space.

Using the bulk helper performs the indexing correctly:
```
from elasticsearch.helpers import bulk
action = [{
    '_id': '2017-04-14T16:04:45.000000+0000',
    '_index': index,
    '_type': doc_type,
    '_source': body       
}]
bulk(es_client, action)
```

Version: `elasticsearch==5.3.0`

issue_title: Connection timeout if port is unset/implied.
issue_body:
We spent a good amount of time to figure this out yesterday. Our elasticsearch host is proxied by nginx so when we try to access it we use `http://<username>:<password>@<ip_address>` in django-haystack, we get a timeout.

The problem went away when we explicitly set port 80. I'm under the impression that the problem is in elasticsearch-py mostly because the stack trace says so. We've tried using curl and httpie as our elasticsearch client and it worked fine.

I have some changes, but realized while updating the tests that it might be possible to access elasticsearch with another scheme. So I need feedback before continuing.

Is it safe to assume that es is only served either via http or https? I also don't know what the expected behavior is if the protocol/scheme is not included(example.com/_count instead of https://example.com/_count).

issue_title: x-ms-client-request-id causes Interactive Mode / ScenarioTest scenarios to fail
issue_body:
Consider the following commands:
```
az network lb create -g myrg -n lb
az network lb inbound-nat-rule create -g myrg --lb-name lb -n rule1 --frontend-port 100 --backend-port 100 --protocol tcp
az network lb inbound-nat-rule create -g myrg --lb-name lb -n rule2 --frontend-port 200 --backend-port 200 --protocol tcp
```

This creates a load balancer with two inbound NAT rules. This scenario runs just fine if you use the CLI normally, and it runs fine in the current scenario test (which uses the `ResourceGroupVCRTestBase` class).

However, if you run these commands in interactive mode, or you run it as part of a `ScenarioTest` based test, the call to create the second rule will fail, stating that the second rule does not exist. Examination via the portal confirms this as well.

- [X] Remove x-ms-client-id from LiveTest and ScenarioTest base (PR #3655)
- [x] Generate new x-ms-client-id in `az interactive` for each new command (PR #3435)

cc/ @troydai, @oakeyc

---

issue_title: Invalid cloud configurations leaves CLI in corrupted state.
issue_body:
### Description
If you use attempt to use an incorrectly configured cloud
```
az cloud register -n Dogfood --endpoint-resource-manager https://api-dogfood.resources.windows-int.net/ --endpoint-management https://management.core.windows.net/ --endpoint-active-directory https://login.windows-ppe.net --endpoint-active-directory-graph-resource-id https://graph.ppe.windows.net/ 
```

After login, the CLI won't load:
```
Error loading command module 'acr'
Error loading command module 'acs'
Error loading command module 'appservice'
