
I deleted `~/.azure/clouds.config`, to attempt to recover, but now the CLI reports the cloud being invalid:
```
CloudNotRegisteredException: The cloud 'Dogfood' is not registered.
```

but I cannot list or change clouds.

### Setup
A VM or VMSS that has a cloud-init config defined in `virtualMachineProfile.osProfile.customData` (or any other data).

### Issue
Running the command `az vm show --resource-group <rg> --name <name>` or `az vmss show --resource-group <rg> --name <name>` will return a `null` value for `virtualMachineProfile.osProfile.customData`.

Any subsequent attempts to update this value with the command `az vmss update --resource-group <rg> --name <name> --set virtualMachineProfile.osProfile.customData=<val>` will leave the resulting value shown as `null`.

I was able to verify that the initial custom data (a cloud config) was handed over to the VM and that after an update, my updated cloud config was handed over.  So I am certain that the value of `customData` is not null, however, the azure-cli does not show this way.

issue_title: [Versioning] Mechanism for specifying enum values is too cumbersome
issue_body:
Unresolved from PR #2526 

```Python
def create_storage_account(..., 
   kind=get_versioned_models(ResourceType.MGMT_STORAGE_STORAGE_ACCOUNTS, model='Kind').storage.value, ...):
```

We should implement a way of specifying a default enum type as a value that doesn't require so much boilerplate syntax and, ideally, completely hides the versioning aspect.

issue_title: How to get IP address from a VM

issue_body:
With increasing frequency, the issue crops up where PRs tend to fail on the CI's version check. Usually this manifests as nearly all modules complaining about their versions. People "merge" the latest changes from dev (because that's generally what the Git community recommends) and then the CI still fails for the same reason. At that point, the author says "I've done all I can," and reaches out to us to make things right.

The solution for this seems to always be for them to rebase their branch against the latest in dev, *not* to merge dev into their branch. If we could have a git bot that would do this, or a button that appears that would automatically do this, it would help, especially for help-only PRs where the author is likely not familiar with Git and may only be submitting changes through GitHub.

issue_title: Cannot define Batch start task user identity (admin/non-admin)
issue_body: In the Azure Portal you can define whether the start task should run as admin or not. The `az batch pool create` command doesn't seem to support that. It makes certain scenarios impossible.

issue_title: storage command help text is not helpful at all
issue_body:
The help text for most storage commands is terrible: it isn't actionable, describes internal (SDK) semantics, and generally provide the information needed to actually call the command.  See, for example:
`az storage share create --help`

`  az storage share create: Creates a new share under the specified account.
        If the share with the same name already exists, the operation fails on the service. By
        default, the exception is swallowed by the client. To expose the exception, specify True for
        fail_on_exists`

Clearly, this text is relevant to an SDK consumer but not a CLI user.  Moreover, there are no examples and it's nearly impossible to make sense of the parameter combos that are valid.

I'm seeing random TLS/SSL CERTIFICATE_VERIFY_FAILED  errors on calls to azure api via an explicit proxy. We are using ansible to make various calls to the api from inside our azure deployment. Direct outbound access is blocked and we route via an explicit proxy.

"Traceback (most recent call last):\n  File \"/tmp/ansible_OyZENy/ansible_module_azure_rm_securitygroup_facts.py\", line 286, in <module>\n    main()\n  File \"/tmp/ansible_OyZENy/ansible_module_azure_rm_securitygroup_facts.py\", line 283, in main\n    AzureRMSecurityGroupFacts()\n  File \"/tmp/ansible_OyZENy/ansible_module_azure_rm_securitygroup_facts.py\", line 236, in __init__\n    facts_module=True)\n  File \"/tmp/ansible_OyZENy/ansible_modlib.zip/ansible/module_utils/azure_rm_common.py\", line 173, in __init__\n  File \"/usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages/msrestazure/azure_active_directory.py\", line 403, in __init__\n    self.set_token()\n  File \"/usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages/msrestazure/azure_active_directory.py\", line 434, in set_token\n    raise_with_traceback(AuthenticationError, \"\", err)\n  File \"/usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages/msrest/exceptions.py\", line 50, in raise_with_traceback\n    raise error\nmsrest.exceptions.AuthenticationError: , SSLError: [SSL: CERTIFICATE_VERIFY_FAILED] certificate verify failed (_ssl.c:579)\n", "module_stdout": "", "msg": "MODULE FAILURE"}

issue_title: insert_or_replace_entity(tableservice) raise an error when the RowKey is an empty string
issue_body:
Even though it can be done. I did it before with the .net WindowsAzure.Storage nuget.
Of course this issue reoccurs related to all of the table 'insert' actions.

[https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/rest/api/storageservices/designing-a-scalable-partitioning-strategy-for-azure-table-storage](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/rest/api/storageservices/designing-a-scalable-partitioning-strategy-for-azure-table-storage#yyy)

### Which service(blob, file, queue) does this issue concern?
blob

### What problem was encountered?
[These constants](https://github.com/Azure/azure-storage-python/blob/master/azure-storage-blob/azure/storage/blob/blockblobservice.py#L93) are too low for the current version (which is [`2017-07-29`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-storage-python/blob/8d35972169035b5be96b90f659b11f725a2738bf/azure-storage-blob/azure/storage/blob/_constants.py#L11))

### Have you found a mitigation/solution?
The constants should be changed to the following:

- `MAX_SINGLE_PUT_SIZE = 256 * 1024 *1024` ([ref](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/rest/api/storageservices/put-blob#remarks))
- `MAX_BLOCK_SIZE = 100 * 1024 * 1024` ([ref](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/rest/api/storageservices/put-block))
- `MIN_LARGE_BLOCK_UPLOAD_THRESHOLD = 100 * 1024 * 1024 + 1` to match `MAX_BLOCK_SIZE`
The styling of the [pydocumentdb documentation](http://azure.github.io/azure-documentdb-python/) appears to be broken.

issue_title: PRI 0: Use the Offset parameter in append to ensure we are always appending at the offset we expect
issue_body:
The append API allows for a query parameter called offset, which allows you to specify where you think the end of the file is to append data to. This is very useful for us, since it allows us to submit an append call where we "think" the file should currently be. If data has already been uploaded at that location, you will receive a 400 error with a BadOffsetException, which you can catch. This will indicate that data is already at that location and we can move on to the next 4mb chunk to upload. This will help preserve the uploaded file and ensure that its final length is the same as the source file.

Here is a sample request/response where the offset is not the end of the file (meaning there is already data at or ahead of the offset specified):

**Request:**

issue_title: Better conform support for complex addresses, with localization
issue_body:
The [addition of an Estonian data source](https://github.com/openaddresses/openaddresses/pull/1076) has surfaced some problems with how our current conform processing language doesn't fully capture addresses. See also [much discussion in this issue](https://github.com/openaddresses/openaddresses/issues/1077) about mapping. I suspect this same issue comes up for other whole-country sources like Denmark and the Netherlands.

Long story short, this example could motivate us improving the quality of the data we are extracting and publishing. I think the best way to make progress would be to get input from people who are experts in actually using this data in geocoders and know something about different locales addressing. End goal: for us to publish a CSV file that is directly useful.

This issue is deliberately broad and open ended, it's really a product discussion more than an engineering question. I imagine it will only be solved with a collection of several small coding projects.

## Long story short

With recent 1.3 release we start to receive this kind of crash report. 

```
AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'cancel'
  File "gns3server/controller/compute.py", line 502, in _run_http_query
    response = yield from self._session().request(method, url, headers=headers, data=data, auth=self._auth, chunked=chunked, timeout=timeout)
  File "aiohttp/client.py", line 583, in __iter__
    resp = yield from self._coro
  File "aiohttp/client.py", line 279, in _request
    resp.connection.add_callback(handle.cancel)

AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'cancel'
```
The problem start with this commit: https://github.com/aio-libs/aiohttp/commit/4be504377daa3c535c488ad30bd50be48ffedeb7

The problem seem to arrive when we pass timeout=None to request. But it doesn't seem to be always the case.

The problem in _request is if you pass None to the TimeoutHandle you will get a None handle
```python
# timeout is cumulative for all request operations
# (request, redirects, responses, data consuming)
tm = TimeoutHandle(timeout)
handle = tm.handle(self._loop)
```

And we will use this handle later:
```python
        if resp.connection is not None:
            resp.connection.add_callback(handle.cancel)
        else:
            handle.cancel()
```

The code of handle in TimeoutHandle:
```python
    def handle(self, loop):
        if self._timeout is not None and self._timeout > 0:
            at = ceil(loop.time() + self._timeout)
            return loop.call_at(at, self.__call__)
```


Is it wrong to pass None as a timeout?

QUIC takes about 30% traffic on hotspots. Generaly Google Ads.

```
async def write(self, chunk, *, drain=True, LIMIT=0x10000):
...

...
    if chunk:

            chunk_len = ('%x\r\n' % len(chunk)).encode('ascii')
```

```
async def write_eof(self, chunk=b''):

```

Windows\Linux

issue_title: Cancel on waiting jobs should be protected
issue_body:
Example:

```python
from aiohttp import web
from aiojobs.aiohttp import setup, spawn

async def handler(request):
    await spawn(request, coro())
    return web.Response()

app = web.Application()
app.router.add_get('/', handler)
setup(app)
```

Cancellation of `await spawn(request, coro())` by https://github.com/aio-libs/aiohttp/pull/2257/files should be covered by shield or custom wrapper
Can this get added to PyPi so it can be pip installed?

## Problem

DynamoDB::Query operations fail due to a `UnicodeDecodeError` during response deserialization:
```
UnicodeDecodeError: 'utf-8' codec can't decode byte 0x8b in position 1: invalid start byte
```

An identical query succeeds using regular botocore.

## Discussion

0x8b (139) is outside of the ASCII range and based on some cursory Googling, appears to indicate that the response is gzipped:

* https://stackoverflow.com/questions/11072705/twitter-trends-api-unicodedecodeerror-utf8-codec-cant-decode-byte-0x8b-in-po
* https://github.com/rg3/youtube-dl/issues/366
* https://stackoverflow.com/questions/9445627/urllib2-opener-providing-wrong-charset

Related to #258?

## Minimal reproducer

```python
import asyncio
from pprint import pprint

import aiobotocore
import botocore.session


QUERY_KWARGS = {
    'ExpressionAttributeNames': {
        '#key': 'key',
        '#seq': 'seq',
        '#stream': 'stream',
        '#timestamp': 'timestamp',
        '#value': 'value',
    },
    'ExpressionAttributeValues': {
        ':stream': {'S': 'channel/1139320d29cb42e9813be24d363bc464/content'},
    },
    'IndexName': 'seq',
    'KeyConditionExpression': '#stream = :stream',
    'ProjectionExpression': '#seq, #key, #value, #timestamp',
    'ScanIndexForward': False,
    'TableName': 'flow-orbit-stage-events',
}


def query_botocore():
    session = botocore.session.get_session()

    ddb = session.create_client('dynamodb')
    paginator = ddb.get_paginator('query')
    page_iterator = paginator.paginate(**QUERY_KWARGS)

    for page in page_iterator:
        pprint(page)


async def query_aiobotocore():
    session = aiobotocore.get_session()

    async with session.create_client('dynamodb') as ddb:
        paginator = ddb.get_paginator('query')
        page_iterator = paginator.paginate(**QUERY_KWARGS)

        async for page in page_iterator:
            pprint(page)


if __name__ == '__main__':
    print('Running query with vanilla botocore')
    query_botocore()

    print()

    print('Running query with aiobotocore')
    asyncio.get_event_loop().run_until_complete(query_aiobotocore())
```

### Result

```
$ python3 repro.py
Running query with vanilla botocore
{'Count': 6,
 'Items': [{'key': {'S': 'message/2a43dd8b854742bab50699ac6b61a341/reaction/947c284d507443b99802407a56b8e88b'},
            'seq': {'N': '7'},
            'timestamp': {'N': '1505153725.325'},
            'value': {'S': 'blah blah blah'}},

           ... omitted for brevity ...

 ],
 'ResponseMetadata': {'HTTPHeaders': {'connection': 'keep-alive',
                                      'content-length': '4120',
                                      'content-type': 'application/x-amz-json-1.0',
```

issue_body:
As in this closed issue, https://github.com/jupyter/notebook/issues/2392, I am having trouble copy pasting cells between notebooks in chrome, on linux. However, I can't seem to get <ctrl> + c, <ctrl> + v to actually copy the cells for me, as seems to be the solution in that discussion. I do not have this problem in firefox. I also don't have this problem in chrome on a windows computer.

I am running Jupyter 5.0.0. 
Version 61.0.3163.79 (Official Build) Built on Ubuntu , running on Ubuntu 16.04 (64-bit)
Behavior in that system. I highlight several cells with [Esc] + <shift> j + <shift> j
I copy those cells with <ctrl> c
I go to the other notebook and paste those cells with <ctrl> v
The cells don't copy. Any text I had on the clipboard already pastes into those cells.

Behavior in Firefox. I highlight several cells with [Esc] + <shift> j + <shift> j
I copy those cells with <ctrl> c
I go to the other notebook and paste those cells with <ctrl> v.
I get a message about pressing ctrl-v again to paste. Everything pastes.

Behavior in Chrome on windows.

Is copy paste (or jupyter in general), just not supported in chromium, or is this something that can be corrected? Or is there some different keyboard combenation that I can use to get this to work. Thanks!

issue_title: How do I tell many jupyter notebooks where to look for global project resources?
issue_body:
I have a project with, say, a hundred tutorials that is organized roughly like this:

```
    ├── a-dir
    │   ├── sub-dir
    │   │   └── sub-sub-dir
    │   │       ├── tutorial1
    │   │       │   ├── tutorial1.ipynb
    │   │       │   └── assets
    │   │       │       └── ...
    │   │       ├── tutorial2
    │   │       │   ├── tutorial2.ipynb
    │   │       │   └── assets
    │   │       │       └── ...
                .
                .
                .
    └── tests
        ├── test_tutorials.py
        ├── tutorials_utils.py
        ├── custom_extension.py
        └── custom_preprocessor.py
```

I want to run a test suite that iterates through all the tutorials, runs them, converts them to markdown (which will then be displayed on a website), and fails if any notebook raises an error while running.

My tutorials directories each have a subdirectory of assets that are used in the notebooks and the resulting markdown files, so it seems natural to put those files there. However, I also need the tutorial notebooks to be able to load a custom extension I wrote in `custom_extension.py`, and to have access to some helper functions I wrote in  `tutorials_utils.py`.

It seems that all of Jupyter's imports are relative to the directory where the notebook is saved. How can I additionally tell Jupyter to look in the `tests` directory?

I want to do this in two ways:

 1. During automated integration testing, using the Python API for nbconvert or something similar (what exactly is the best way to "just" run a jupyter notebook from the command line?).
 2. During development, i.e., from within the notebook browser GUI. I'm currently loading notebooks with, e.g., `jupyter notebook a-dir/sub-dir/sub-sub-dir/tutorial1/tutorial1.ipynb`. What options would I need to add to this to point it to `tests/`? I tried various values for `--NotebookApp.nbserver_extensions=<Dict>` but I can't figure out the proper syntax for passing a Dict to the CLI

I would prefer not to put a `sys.path.append` line in every single jupyter notebook, since there are so many, and the directory structure may change in the future, requiring hundreds of updates.

> Note, this shouldn't be seen to hold up #879, or as encroachment on anything planned for 5.0

After #879, notebook 4.x users will may have to run between two and three commands (in addition to package installation) for each package they wish to "unpack":

``` shell
jupyter serverextension enable --py the_module # and/or...
jupyter nbextension install  --py the_module # and/or...
jupyter nbextension enable  --py the_module # and/or...
```

This opinion is such to support the freedom that a user has to control what javascript they are served in addition to what python they have... and indeed, in the case of custom widgets, where only the kernel will care where these frontend assets are, and the backend contract still stands.

However, this is somewhat odious for developers who want to make their install as simple as possible for users.

By adding an "enable" member to the nbext path spec, a developer can already inform the user of _their_ preference:

``` shell
jupyter nbextension install --py the_package
> Installing the_package...
> Copying...
> the_ext/index will always load in the `tree` app
```

after specifying: 

``` python
{
  "src": "static",
  "dest": "the_ext",
  "enable": True,
  "require": "the_ext/index
}
```

Similarly, if a user installs something that also has a serverextension, this would be nice:

``` shell
jupyter serverextension install --py the_package
> Configuring...
> the_package contains nbextensions...
> Copying...
> ... the_ext/tree will always load in the `tree` app
> ... the_ext/notebook will always load in the `notebook` app
```

After specifying:

```
{
  "module": "the_package.TheExtension",
  "nbextensions": True
}
```

```python
X = np.random.random((10000, 10000))
```

Currently, the following module versions will be considered as valid by _pylint-odoo_ when using default configuration:

- `10+0.1.0.0`, because the dot from `valid_odoo_versions` is not escaped when building the valid module version pattern (and the `.` inside a regex matches any character other than a newline): https://github.com/OCA/pylint-odoo/blob/a14ddca6f0d57e603c55f60b0128b129ad5fb81e/pylint_odoo/checkers/no_modules.py#L657-L661
- `10.0.1.0.0WHATEVER`, because `re.match` [is used](https://github.com/OCA/pylint-odoo/blob/a14ddca6f0d57e603c55f60b0128b129ad5fb81e/pylint_odoo/checkers/no_modules.py#L661) inside `formatversion()`, which [matches the string from the beginning](https://docs.python.org/2/library/re.html#re.match), however, there is no `$` at the end of the default value for `manifest_version_format` to match the end of the string: https://github.com/OCA/pylint-odoo/blob/a14ddca6f0d57e603c55f60b0128b129ad5fb81e/pylint_odoo/checkers/no_modules.py#L237
Thus, anything can follow once the version is matched from the beginning of the string.

I have started working on a fix, however, I have stumbled into a slight problem for which I need feedback from the authors - if a _.pylintrc_ file is used with `manifest_version_format` defined inside it using the `%(valid_odoo_versions)s` placeholder, then `manifest_version_format` will be set automatically by `ConfigParser` (which [pylint uses for configuration parsing](https://github.com/PyCQA/pylint/blob/e231a409a83a23c24c8b7dbbaca79314eee6d4c6/pylint/config.py#L462)) due to [interpolation](https://docs.python.org/2/library/configparser.html#ConfigParser.ConfigParser). This means that at the point when the `manifest_version_format_parsed` string is built: https://github.com/OCA/pylint-odoo/blob/a14ddca6f0d57e603c55f60b0128b129ad5fb81e/pylint_odoo/checkers/no_modules.py#L657-L661
then `self.config.manifest_version_format` no longer contains the `%(valid_odoo_versions)s` placeholder, as it was already formatted by `ConfigParser`. This becomes a problem if we want to escape the dots from `valid_odoo_versions` (eg. `10.0` -> `10\.0`) inside [`formatversion()`](https://github.com/OCA/pylint-odoo/blob/a14ddca6f0d57e603c55f60b0128b129ad5fb81e/pylint_odoo/checkers/no_modules.py#L657), as our values will be simply ignored if a config file is used with `manifest_version_format` defined inside it using the `%(valid_odoo_versions)s` placeholder.

I have comeup with one solution - switch to [`str.format()`](https://docs.python.org/2/library/stdtypes.html#str.format) style formatting for `manifest_version_format` in the config, which would allow to avoid interpolation by `ConfigParser`. However, I guess it would be a breaking change. So my question is - would it be ok?

Maybe someone has other suggestions?

issue_title: Rewrite React-components for new lifecycle-functions in v16
issue_body:
From React v16.0 the following lifecycle-functions are unsafe due to the new render-pipeline:
 - `componentWillMount()`
 - `componentWillReceiveProps()`
 - `componentWillUpdate()`

And the new lifecycle function `static getDerivedStateFromProps(nextProps, prevState)` was introduced.

This means we need to go through all existing `React.Component`s and remove all usage of the unsafe lifecycle-functions.

More info about React components can be found in the Component-doc [here](https://reactjs.org/docs/react-component.html), and changelog for React v16.0 is [here](https://reactjs.org/blog/2017/09/26/react-v16.0.html)

issue_title: grad of anp.abs and anp.absolute returning NaN when input is zero
issue_body:
anp.abs and anp.absolute return NaN when one of the inputs is identically zero:
>> grad(anp.abs)(0.0)

Note that anp.fabs does not have this issue.

The reason is the division by ans in the definition of the abs grad:
anp.abs.defgrad(     lambda ans, x : lambda g : g * anp.conj(x) / ans)

https://github.com/HIPS/autograd/blob/master/autograd/numpy/numpy_grads.py#L96


I'm not sure what is the best way to fix this, as naive solutions involve assigning to an array which would break when taking higher order derivatives.

issue_title: best practice for starting consumers for each topic
issue_body:
I am trying to implement nsq consumers for different topics for our application. The way I am designing this is to have a separate process for each topic. And, for each process, have several instances of Reader instantiated. Something like this:

        consumers = [test_consumer1, test_consumer2, .., ..]
        pool_size = len(consumers)
        pool = mp.Pool(pool_size)

        try:
            for consumer in consumers:
                pool.apply_async(consumer)

def test_consumer():
    for i in range(5):
          Reader(
            topic=self.topic,
            channel=self.channel,
            message_handler=self.message_handler,
            max_in_flight=5,
            lookupd_http_addresses=self.lookupd_http_addresses
        )  
    nsq.run()

The reasoning behind this is to have one IOLoop instance and and 5 different reader instances for each process. Thanks,
Aravindh

issue_title: Ability to post files from a local directory instead of URL
issue_body:
more a feature request...

Here is how I had to implement in my legacy library using toolbelt... (https://pypi.python.org/pypi/requests-toolbelt)

```python
from requests_toolbelt.multipart.encoder import MultipartEncoder

def post_localfile(at, roomId, filename, text='', toPersonId='', toPersonEmail=''):
    openfile = open(filename, 'rb')
    filename = ntpath.basename(filename)
    payload = {'roomId': roomId, 'files': (filename, openfile, 'image/jpg')}
    if text:
        payload['text'] = text
    if toPersonId:
        payload['toPersonId'] = toPersonId
    if toPersonEmail:
        payload['toPersonEmail'] = toPersonEmail
    m = MultipartEncoder(fields=payload)
    headers = {'Authorization': _fix_at(at), 'Content-Type': m.content_type}
    resp = requests.request("POST",url=_url('/messages'), data=m, headers=headers)
    file_dict = json.loads(resp.text)
    file_dict['statuscode'] = str(resp.status_code)
return file_dict
```

issue_title: The reason behind disabling the plugin by default
issue_body:
Please see my description of my previous problem [here](https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/issues/2992).

I wanted to ask why do we have to explicitly enable this plugin? Based on what's written [here](https://docs.pytest.org/en/2.7.3/plugins.html?highlight=re#external-plugins):

```If a plugin is installed, pytest automatically finds and integrates it, there is no need to activate it. ```

But having to activate this plugin separately goes against this.

issue_title: Correct way to set up a Test Suite
issue_body:
Hi!

The test suite I am working on is set up this way:
Several .py files testing different sections of the web application
A test_app.py that imports all other .py files
pytest is called on that last file to execute the whole test suite
```
pytest test_app.py --driver Firefox
```

As a result each function ends up opening a new browser window. I was expecting only one instance reused on the whole test suite.

I am experiencing a constant degrading on the app I am testing and I would like to rule out a problem with the test suite setup.

Is this setup the correct way to use pytest-selenium?

I have a few other pytest testsuites where I set up the use of selenium myself, so this is my first time using pytest selenium.

I know this is a pretty general question so I thank you in advance for your kindness.

[debian changelog](https://launchpad.net/ubuntu/+source/netcat-openbsd/1.130-3)

When using updated version of netcat (found problem on arch) py.test will indefinitely wait for "echo 'flush_all' | nc 127.0.0.1 11211" "echo 'flush_all' | nc -N 127.0.0.1 11211" is needed for the old behaviour which is not compatible with previous versions like ubuntu 14.04.

Created a pull request which uses pylibmc to flush_all and remove the dependency on nc

I have pytest.ini with `addopts = --instafail`.
I have other python program which must collect list of tests. Simple solution [here](https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/issues/2039).
To disable pytest output I use argument `-p no:terminal` as suggested above.
The result is `AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'config'`
This happens because there is no terminalreporter plugin and `None` is returned here https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest-instafail/blob/55bb4d392867e3270a27dd0e6faa0c83441284a2/pytest_instafail.py#L32

I just installed django-oscar on my Django 1.9 application
i have an existing application i.e. user model etc and everything
i have managed to get it working but only one issue is the dashboard admin navigation menu is empty.
the user i am logged in as is an admin and superuser and staff etc.

anything else i am missing?
from shell i can see that settings.OSCAR_DASHBOARD_NAVIGATION is filled with some values i.e. it is not empty?

Since `SlugField` supports unicode since Django 1.9 (https://docs.djangoproject.com/es/1.9/ref/models/fields/#django.db.models.SlugField.allow_unicode), which is already supported by latest Oscar, we should probably think about proper unicode slugs support in the Oscar too.

Firstly, we could make optional conversion from unicode to ASCII in the slugify function - https://github.com/django-oscar/django-oscar/blob/master/src/oscar/core/utils.py#L41.

Also, perhaps there's no need in custom `oscar.models.fields.AutoSlugField`, since original one from `django_extensions` already support custom slugify function - https://github.com/django-extensions/django-extensions/blob/master/django_extensions/db/fields/__init__.py#L109.

On March 2nd, i asked this on Slack:

> Kees Hink [2:56 PM]
> How would one model a catalogue where all Products of a certain ProductClass are available in all AttributeOptions, and at the same price? Does one have to add all variants manually?
> 
> To take the sandbox as an example: All “T-shirt” products are now available in all Colors, and they all cost the same. So when i add a new T-shirt product, i don’t want to have to add Variants for each individual Color: there might be 20, that’s a lot of work. 
> (For now let’s disregard the “T-shirt size” factor by saying the new product is a size L. But bonus points if there’s a way to also let the customer pick any size as well, without having to add these manually.)
> 
> Is there a shortcut here?
> 
> Kees Hink [3:24 PM]
> I think product.options does what i want. Small issues:
> - I don’t seem to be able to set that through the dashboard (need django-admin)
> - It displays a string input field instead of the desired “colors” dropdown
> But that’s just implementation details, the basics are there!

Now that i've implemented this (https://www.barriquewijnhandel.nl/catalogue/category/glitterfles_7/), i thought i'd report what i had to do to get it working:

- Fork Oscar catalogue
- Add Color model + migration
- Fork Oscar's basket app.
- Override Oscar's SimpleAddToBasketForm
- Modify the AddToBasketForm to show a choice widget instead of text
- Test that we can manage custom colors for adding a Glitter product to basket
- Use Color objects in add to basket form
- Customize dashboard catalogue nav: also manage colors
- Make Colors manageable in Oscar Dashboard

Is this something we want to make easier? If so, i could share the knowledge, or make a PR or something.

issue_title: Hosting Bundle installer removes RC version of 2.1.0, breaking all RC apps
issue_body:
# Issue Title

The Hosting Bundle Installer of .NET Core 2.1.0 removes the 2.1.0-rc1 version of the ASP.NET Core shared frameworks (shared framework M.NetCore.App 2.1.0-rc1 is still left), thus breaking every ASP.NET Core 2.1.0-rc1 app on the system since there is no roll-forward from RC to RTM.

# Steps to reproduce

1. Install hosting bundle installer for 2.1.0-rc1
2. Deploy 2.1.0-rc1 apps to the system (e.g. using `Microsoft.AspNetCore.All` sharedfx)
3. Install hosting bundle installer for 2.1.0 RTM
4. Observe that the 2.1.0-rc1 apps break and are no longer able to run.

# Expected behavior

The RTM hosting bundle shall not uninstall previous version of the shared framework for which there is no roll-forward logic in hostfxr.

issue_title: Site verification fails of acme-challenge file ends with newline
issue_body:
Using the `letsencrypt-auto` command in manual mode requires to create a challenge file under `.well-known/acme-challange`.

The file content should be the line printed out by the tool on the command line and be serves as "text/plain".

However if the file contains a newline, the validation fails.

Creating this file manually with an editor like `vi` automatically adds a newline to the end of the file.

IMHO this should be fixed to trim the string read from the web server before comparing.

cf https://auth0.com/blog/2015/03/31/critical-vulnerabilities-in-json-web-token-libraries/

I don't think the current JOSE implementation supports the 'None' alg type, but it does support HMAC and RSA. If the verifier isn't explicitly checking for the one that it's expecting, an attacker could potentially use this to construct valid payloads.

The blog post recommends:

> The server should already know what algorithm it uses to sign tokens, and it's not safe to allow attackers to provide this value.
> Some might argue that some servers need to support more than one algorithm for compatibility reasons. In this case, a separate key can (and should) be used for each supported algorithm. JWT conveniently provides a "key ID" field (kid) for exactly this purpose. Since servers can use the key ID to look up the key and its corresponding algorithm, attackers are no longer able to control the manner in which a key is used for verification. In any case, I don't think JWT libraries should even look at the alg field in the header, except maybe to check that it matches what was the expected algorithm.

issue_body:
It occurs to me from looking at crsmithdev/arrow#72 that it might be useful to have a `resolve_imaginary` function built in to `dateutil`, similar to this one that I wrote as an example:

```python
from dateutil.tz import datetime_exists
from datetime import timedelta

def resolve_imaginary(dt):
    if dt.tzinfo is not None and not datetime_exists(dt):
        # You need to shift this by the difference between the current offset
        # and the previous one. Historically, there has never been a time zone
        # shift of more than 24 hours, nor has there been two changes to a time
        # zone within 24 hours, so if we take the "wall time" - 24 hours, we
        # should be OK, barring any insane time zone behavior in the future.
        curr_offset = dt.utcoffset()
        old_offset = (dt - timedelta(hours=24)).utcoffset()

        dt += curr_offset - old_offset

    return dt
```

I'm fairly certain that an imaginary date you always want to "slide forward" - it will never be that you accidentally wanted to go back.

We have a particular advantage here because, like with `datetime_ambiguous` and `datetime_exists`, we can define an API in the `tzinfo` objects themselves that lets us avoid the "look back 24 hours" heuristic if at all possible. This works for all known transitions in the Olson database, but we could get a sudden breakage if, for example, a specific zone changes their DST and then changes back within 24 hours, or something like that.

issue_title: FLT3 Large Insertion Generating Incorrect HGVS_P
issue_body: I found a curious situation when trying to annotator a large insertion in FLT3. This insertion of about ~100 basepairs seems to produce a hgvs_p signifying a delins affecting nearly 400 amino acid codons. I am at a loss for why this would happen. Any context or explanation would be greatly appreciated. Official HGVS guidelines state that "in-frame insertions containing a translation stop codon in the inserted sequence are described as an insertion, not as a deletion-insertion removing the entire C-terminal amino acid sequence." Information about this can be found[ here](http://varnomen.hgvs.org/recommendations/protein/variant/insertion/). Example code snippets are below. `var_c = hp.parse_hgvs_variant('NM_004119.2:c.1756_1757insGTGACCGGCTCCTCAGATAATGAGTACTTCTACGTTGATTTCAGAGAATATGAATATGATCTCAAATGGGAGTTTCCAAGAGAAAATTTAGAGTTTGAG')` `vm.c_to_p(var_c)` Output: `Out[2]: SequenceVariant(ac=NP_004110.2, type=p, posedit=(Asp586_Ser993delinsGlyAspArgLeuLeuArg))`

issue_title: Client: Don't use cache by default
issue_body:
Client tries to be overly helpful by caching by default. This is handy functionality, but users should enable this themselves.

Either disable by default, or consider renaming `cache_path = dir` to `cache = False|True|str`, where False is no caching, True is default path (current behavior), and str is custom path.

Currently, the `show` method is the only way to get a plot from SFrame/SArray, and the plot immediately opens in a native GUI app.

The resulting plot object could also be:
* shown in native GUI (`.show()`)
* saved to a file (`.save()`)
* combined with other plot objects into a tiled view (perhaps `tc.show(a, b, c, d)` where a,b,c,d are plot objects)

issue_title: Replace libiomp.so soft link with a configurable path in native project
issue_body:
When build native project, libiomp.so will be added into the jar package. Currently, the libiomp.so is checkin to the code base with a soft link point to /opt/intel/... This is bad, because 1. there will be no error if intel mkl is not installed into that path. The error can't find libiomp.so will be thrown in runtime; 2. the path can't be configurable.

So I think we should use a configurable path(stored in env var or java property) to replace the soft link file. The default value can be /opt/intel/..., but it will throw error in compile time when can't find the so file and user can modify that value when the intel mkl is installed in another place in their box.

```
[ERROR] 
```

To reproduce this error message, you can check out project [SampleMlp](https://github.com/qiuxin2012/SampleMlp), and change 1.5.1 to 1.6.3

In the distributed optimizer, an epoch ends based on the following line:

````scala
if (accumulateCount >= dataset.size()) {
  ...
}
````

This does not make sense to me. `accumulateCount` is a var that is reset every epoch, and is updated via `accumulateCount += recordsNum.value`. Here, `recordsNum` is an accumulator which keeps track of the number of **training samples** that were used to train in each _iteration_. So, the stopping criterion above is comparing something with units of samples, to something with units of batches. 

Take a case where you have a batch size of 256, and your distributed dataset has 1000 minibatches in total. In this case, each epoch will consist of training on only four minibatches out of 1000, since `256 * 4 > 1000`. However, most of the time an epoch is defined as making a single pass through all of the mini-batches. 

One problem is that there doesn't seem to be any documentation on what the intended behavior is, but I'm fairly confident this is not it.

issue_title: critical error in singlediode p_mp calculation
issue_body:
Bad news. I discovered a major error in the singlediode p_mp calculation: given a time series input, the numerical solver exits if the solution for any single time is within the error tolerance, rather than requiring that the solution for all times be close enough. The error is in [this line](https://github.com/pvlib/pvlib-python/blob/5218c9d3dd2d5a4e410140de3180b7ef00e8b1ee/pvlib/pvsystem.py#L1728). 

Here's some sample code:

```python
photocurrent = np.linspace(0, 10, 11)
resistance_shunt = 16
resistance_series = 0.094
nNsVth = 0.473
saturation_current = 1.943e-09

sd = pvsystem.singlediode(photocurrent, saturation_current, resistance_series, resistance_shunt, nNsVth)

sd['i_mp']
array([ 0.        ,  0.59453544,  1.56145498,  2.54105091,  3.52353436,
        4.50658749,  5.4890739 ,  6.47016579,  7.44909413,  8.42504244,
        9.39708533])

# change .all() to .any()

pvsystem = reload(pvlib.pvsystem)

sd_any = pvsystem.singlediode(photocurrent, saturation_current, resistance_series, resistance_shunt, nNsVth)

sd_any['i_mp']
array([ 0.        ,  0.54538398,  1.43273966,  2.36328163,  3.29255606,
        4.23101358,  5.16177031,  6.09368251,  7.02197553,  7.96846051,
        8.88220557])

sd['i_mp']/sd_any['i_mp']
array([        nan,  1.09012266,  1.0898386 ,  1.07522137,  1.07015167,
        1.06513189,  1.06340917,  1.06178256,  1.06082599,  1.05729864,
        1.05796756])
```

So the error here is on the order of 5 to 10%, but the results will depend on your exact input.

The blue dots on the IV curve below show the p_mp points in the old code. The black dots show the p_mp points in fixed code. These IV curves are generated using a fix for #83 that I'll make a PR for soon. They're also what led me to discovering the problem.

![ivcurves](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/4383303/17006598/cedaa96c-4e96-11e6-9e9a-64ed1377536c.png) 

On a related note, someone should put more effort into verifying that the matlab and python libraries give the same results. Not me.

issue_title: id field set on ModelSerializer is bypassed and not used
issue_body:
We noticed that the `id = serializers.SerializerMethodField()` that we have set on our `ModelSerializer` is not being when this package creates the json-api structure. Upon investigating I saw this method which appears to use the `resource_object.pk` for the `id` field:

https://github.com/django-json-api/django-rest-framework-json-api/blob/develop/rest_framework_json_api/renderers.py#L387

This causes a bug where if someone has written a custom serialized `id` field it is bypassed completely. In our case we are customizing the id of our model for the client.

We may create a shortcut locally but I think this package should respect anything defined in the local serializers and use that rather than bypassing it. It also brings up a question on using this package in general: does this package change the way fields must be defined compared to the typical DRF way? Does it automatically translate the normal DRF serializer fields into the appropriate json-api versions? I realize it's somewhat complex so just trying to gauge how this will affect writing serializers for our project

issue_title: ResourceRelatedField is too rigid for type validation
issue_body:
Opening this on behalf of @martinmaillard (https://github.com/django-json-api/django-rest-framework-json-api/issues/122#issuecomment-146879198)

`ResourceRelatedField` is way too rigid when it checks the valid type. It gets the expected type from the queryset, which means that:
- it's not possible to customize the type of a relation (what is allowed by the resource_name on the serializer)
- it's not possible to allow multiple types to be valid for one relation (useful with polymorphic relationships)

Currently it seems like the 'date' of a track is mapped to the `TDRC` frame (recording date) instead of `TDRL` (release date).

This can be seen in a few places:
 - The [mid3v2 docs](https://github.com/quodlibet/mutagen/blob/ddf22e36b0ae9afff8a6ed4ba08439451a5af748/docs/man/mid3v2.rst#options)

Is this a bug? Or was this done on purpose for compatibility reasons?

For reference, excerpts from the [id3v2.4 spec](http://id3.org/id3v2.4.0-frames):
 - `TDRC`: "Recording time [...] when the audio was recorded".
 - `TDRL`: "Release time [...] when the audio was first released"
 - `TDOR`: "Original release time [...] when the original recording of the audio was released"

We are a bunch of people working on a symbolic execution engine, and we are intensively depending on Z3 (needless to say, it's really good :-) ). Now we are looking into possibilities of forking Z3 solvers. Basically we'd like to use the incremental solving support in Z3, instead of solving all constraints again once an execution path branches. We looked into code, and found out much effort is required to make Z3 do what we need. What do you think the best option is?

issue_title: Feature request: expose finite sorts to the Java API
issue_body: There's currently no way to create finite sorts from Java, so all finite types must be encoded as bitvectors sorts of some size, and constants must be arbitrary bitvector constants.  Since this is already implemented at the C++ level, it would be great if this was exposed to Java.

We've been debugging some strange behavior in F* and minimized it to something really small:
```
(set-option :smt.mbqi false)
(push)
(assert (not (exists ((@x2 Int)) (= 42 @x2) )))
(check-sat)
```
Without the `(push)` in the second line z3 4.4.1 returns `unsat` on this, but with the `(push)` it returns `unknown`. This change of behavior seems really strange to us, is there any good reason for it?

issue_title: Loading of binary with lots of symbols is slow (round 2)
issue_body:
This is the continuation of this issue:  https://github.com/angr/cle/issues/44

After @rhelmot's fix, the loading time of my example reduced to 17 sec.

But this commit https://github.com/angr/angr/commit/b614e655360e8186bfd33592d1374aaf43ccb46d, increases the loading time to 70 sec.
when I use idabin to load binary, it  reports this error. Could you tell me what's the matter

Hi,

I am analyzing a obfuscated binary that contains the following x86 code:

```
adc cx, di
rcr cl, 0x7b 
```

But my path ends as _errored_:

```
(Pdb) pg.errored[0].error
ClaripyOperationError("args' length must all be equal",)
```

From my understanding this is due to line 311 (function `pc_actions_ADC`) in `simuvex/vex/ccall.py`:
`arg_r = cc_dep2 ^ old_c`
In particular, the _extra check_ (`length_same_check`) performed on the arguments of operator `^` fails. By adding a breakpoint in `pc_actions_ADC()`, I can see that `cc_dep2` is 16 bits, while `cc_ndep` (`old_c = cc_ndep & data[platform]['CondBitMasks']['G_CC_MASK_C']`) is 32 bits. Interestingly, in `pc_calculate_rdata_all_WRK`:

```
cc_dep1_formal = cc_dep1_formal[nbits-1:0]
cc_dep2_formal = cc_dep2_formal[nbits-1:0]
# TODO: does ndep need to be extracted as well?
```

It seems that the answer should be yes at least in the context of ADC. Am I wrong? 
However, if I do that I have other `ClaripyOperationError`s later on.

@zardus gave me some very helpful information for reporting bugs, I think we should add some of this to `CONTRIBUTING.md`.

```
[04:09:26] <zardus> so the absolute best way for us would be:
[04:09:45] <zardus> 1. create a fork off of angr/binaries and angr/angr
[04:10:01] <zardus> 2. give us a PR with angr/binaries, with the binaries in question
[04:10:23] <zardus> 3. give us a PR for angr/angr, with testcases that trigger the binaries in angr/tests/broken_x.py, angr/tests/broken_y.py, etc
[04:10:37] <zardus> in the testcase format that we have (so the code is in a test_blah function)
[04:10:57] <zardus> that way we can very easily merge that and make the scripts run
[04:11:22] <zardus> ideally, we can just fix the bug and rename broken_x.py to test_x.py without making any changes and it'll all work in our CI
```

As said in [Controlling Execution](http://docs.angr.io/docs/paths.html):
In[1]:branched_left = p.successors[0]
In[2]:branched_right = p.successors[1]
In[3]:assert branched_left.addr != branched_right.addr
In[4]:after_branched_left = branched_left.step()[0]
In[5]:after_branched_right = branched_right.step()[0]

But:
In[6]:merged = branched_left.merge(branched_right)
In[7]:assert merged.addr == branched_left.addr and merged.addr == branched_right.addr

My issue is why does'nt **In[7]** confilict with **In[3]** ?

issue_title: Build of libVEX fails on Mac OS X with default Clang
issue_body:
The fix for #53 added the -malign-double compiler flag. Unfortunately this flag is not supported in the version of Clang (Apple LLVM version 8.0.0 (clang-800.0.42.1)) that ships with Mac OS X Sierra (10.12.3).

Current workarounds are either installing a newer version of Clang that supports the flag or to create a `vex` directory (copy `vex-master` in `pyvex/`) and removing the `-malign-double` flag from `Makefile-gcc`.

Hi!

I plan to use pyvex for a project of mine (http://github.com/joxeankoret/diaphora), basically, to have an intermediate language that can be used to diff programs for different CPUs. Do you have any plan in supporting s390?

This CPU, s390, is actually supported by both VEX and Capstone (SystemZ) so, I guess, that adding it should be as easy as adding a s390 architecture definition python file for the archinfo package.

issue_title: Logic error in claripy z3 min function
issue_body:
Located [here](https://github.com/angr/claripy/blob/master/claripy/backends/backend_z3.py#L687).
The return statement causes `extra_constraints` to not be removed from the z3 solver.

Also, consider using the z3 [optimize](https://z3prover.github.io/api/html/classz3py_1_1_optimize.html) object as it works quite well and will probably out perform your multiple evals.

issue_title: [Question|Compatibility] get_changes_since() times out when used through a thread
issue_body:
Hey,
First of all, my apologies for what's probably a stupid question/remark.. :/  


**Behold the following code:**
```python
from steam import SteamClient
from threading import Thread


class machine():

    def __init__(self):
        self.client = SteamClient()
        self.client.anonymous_login()

    def __call__(self):
        if not self.client.connected:
            self.client.anonymous_login()

        return self.test()

    def test(self):
        #Creating a client here, does work..
        result = self.client.get_changes_since(3203225, app_changes=False, package_changes=True)

        if result is None:
            print("TimeOut..")
        else:
            print('Success!')


def main():

    # Thread Test..
    reese = Thread(target=finch)
    reese.start()
    reese.join()

    # Normal Test..
    finch()


finch = machine()
main()
```

When running through a Thread, you'll notice that it'll timeout..  

Again, probably a stupid question..
So let's close this issue in record time, shall we? :)  

Thanks :)

Perhaps this is an improper usage of change_status but I have taken one the basic login examples and after the 4 `print` lines thrown in:

`client.change_status(player_name="new_username")`

However nothing changes, nothing errors, and the application exits on the example `client.logout()` as if everything worked fine. Am I misusing this function or am I missing something here?

Whenever we define a new `GangaObject` subclass we have to also give it a `_schema` data member with an appropriate `Version` argument. As far as I can tell, the only place this version is used is in the VStreamer parsing at https://github.com/ganga-devs/ganga/blob/0b796886d15f843d38e1a85bbb6078f0677dca93/python/Ganga/Core/GangaRepository/VStreamer.py#L311 where it will create a dummy object if the versions don't match. I'm wondering how much we *actually* use this feature and whether it's worth the effort of having to define a `Version` in every `GangaObject`? I assume the idea is supposed to be that the minor version is increased for every addition to the schema and the major version is increased for every removal.

Looking at `Job` however shows that in 5 years, between [here](https://github.com/ganga-devs/ganga/blob/9c227446c18742684e2abd8fc9160e22c650e2eb/python/Ganga/GPIDev/Lib/Job/Job.py#L104) and [here](https://github.com/ganga-devs/ganga/blob/develop/python/Ganga/GPIDev/Lib/Job/Job.py#L172) there were lots of additions made but the version did not increase once. If we *had* have increased the version number then loading a new job in an old Ganga would fail.

At present, if we load a new job with an old Ganga and then again with a new Ganga then information is lost (e.g. setting `parallel_submit=True` on a `Job` in 6.1.13, loading it with 6.0.44 and then again with 6.1.13 resets it to `False`).

I'm not sure how to fix this or even if we need to? If we remove a schema attribute between version then it seems that it is just silently ignored when loading and if we add a new one then it seems to just use the default value. If this is the case and we're ok with it then I suggest removing the version checking entirely. Perhaps we print a warning if loading a job with new/removed attributes?

issue_title: Make use of ids in Dirac splitting
issue_body:
This is a small issue to remind me to look into some sections of the LHCb/Dirac codebase to do with job submission/splitting as well as datasets and such.

It may be possible to get some performance improvements by checking object instances vs making full object comparisons especially when iterating over large lists.

This is somehow a nicety but there is the potential for some nice performance improvements as a result in some areas if this is done correctly.

Hi all,

From memory there is a good reason about having the ```"Done"``` and ```"Complete"``` status in Dirac marked as ```completed``` in ganga.
(I think this was something to do with data replication etc...)

However, when I run my branch #430 I'm seeing jobs consistently being marked as failed as the job is not getting the output file until some time after the job has finished.

After digging around I found my jobs were passing through the monitoring cycle too quickly and were completing whilst Dirac was still processing the output from the Job.

This is responsible for at least some of the problems of jobs completing ahead of the output sandbox or data being made available.

In interests of getting something working I'm keen to move to have only Dirac jobs which are in a ```"Done"``` state (in DIRAC) marked ```completed``` (in Ganga).

Alternatively I'm going to move to mark all jobs in the ```"Complete"``` state but expect some output data in the form of a DiracFile (aka. asking Dirac to upload some output for us) as being in the running state. This is the only way to get Ganga to wait for the output data to be made available as requesting it before it's been uploaded will _work_ and will get an empty output data set which has been a pain to debug as the data exists in DIRAC and could be manually requested in Ganga but was missing after the monitoring loop progressed a job to completed.

Does anyone have any opinions on this?

Thanks,

Rob

The code failed because of a fatal error:
    Invalid status code '500' from http://localhost:8998/sessions with error payload: "com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.exc.UnrecognizedPropertyException: Unrecognized field \"heartbeatTimeoutInSecond\" (class com.cloudera.livy.server.interactive.CreateInteractiveRequest), not marked as ignorable (14 known properties: \"executorCores\", \"conf\", \"driverMemory\", \"name\", \"driverCores\", \"pyFiles\", \"archives\", \"queue\", \"kind\", \"executorMemory\", \"files\", \"jars\", \"proxyUser\", \"numExecutors\"])\n at [Source: HttpInputOverHTTP@40fe6bf7; line: 1, column: 52](through reference chain: com.cloudera.livy.server.interactive.CreateInteractiveRequest["heartbeatTimeoutInSecond"])".

Some things to try:
a) Make sure Spark has enough available resources for Jupyter to create a Spark context.
b) Contact your Jupyter administrator to make sure the Spark magics library is configured correctly.
c) Restart the kernel.

issue_title: Colors for live state history legend are not correct
issue_body: ![image](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/2932687/23305119/8ffa9028-fa9d-11e6-85ae-755360529548.png)

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Required Info                         |                                                                |
|---------------------------------|------------------------------------------- |
| Camera Model                       | D415 | 
| Firmware Version                   | 05.08.15.00 | 
| Operating System & Version |   Linux (Ubuntu 16.04.4)  | 
| Kernel Version (Linux Only)    |  4.4.0-119-generic                                 | 
| Platform                                 | PC  |
| SDK Version                            |  v2.10.3                       | 
| Language                            |  C++                       | 


### Issue Description
I'm running the rs-capture example without any modifications. I've tested with two D415, one with original cable and one with original cable + 5m deltaco active usb3.0 extender. 

When I plug in the device I get 2-3 depth frames and then it chrashes, the rgb stream works fine and will continue but depth freeze. If I replug the cable without closing the program it will stream just fine on both devices. 

It seems to me, to get consistent depth stream frame I have to start program, let it freeze (takes 2-3 frames), then take out the USB and replug it. I have patched my drivers with your realsense script (I can get the hardware clock). I have the same issue on a non-supported kernel 4.14. 

It also seems if I get it to work, if I stop the script and start it again, it will hang after a couple of frames. The weird thing is that the RGB stream works fine.

```
class AnimalFilter(django_filters.FilterSet):

    name = django_filters.CharFilter(lookup_type='iexact')

    class Meta:
        model = Animal

    @property
    def qs(self):
        # The query context can be found in self.request.
        return super(AnimalFilter, self).qs.filter(owner=self.request.user)
```

The ` django_filters.CharFilter(lookup_type='iexact')` will give an `TypeError:__init__() got an unexpected keyword argument 'lookup_type'` it should be changed to:

`name = django_filters.CharFilter(lookup_expr=['iexact'])`

Second thing is the function qs  is not really clear on how you get `self.request.user` it mentions "set in context.user" that is not really clear what that is.

There seems to be a bug in type coercion system where an `Int` is not coerced into a `Float`. Here's the code sample that should illustrate the issue:

```python3
from graphql import (
    graphql,
    GraphQLSchema,
    GraphQLObjectType,
    GraphQLField,
    GraphQLArgument,
    GraphQLString,
    GraphQLFloat,
    GraphQLInt,
)

schema = GraphQLSchema(
  query= GraphQLObjectType(
    name='RootQueryType',
    fields={
      'hello': GraphQLField(
        type= GraphQLString,
        args={
          'foo': GraphQLArgument(GraphQLFloat)
        },
        resolver=lambda *_, foo: str(foo)
      )
    }
  ),
  types=[GraphQLInt]
)

# here the coercion of Int -> Float fails
query1 = '''
    query Bad($foo: Int) {
        hello(foo: $foo)
    }
'''

result = graphql(schema, query1, variable_values={'foo': 3})
print(result.data)
print(result.errors)

# here the coercion of Int -> Float works just fine
query2 = '''
    query Good {
        hello(foo: 3)
    }
'''

result = graphql(schema, query2)
print(result.data)
print(result.errors)
```

As far as I can tell from the [spec](http://facebook.github.io/graphql/October2016/#sec-Float), this should fall under input coercion for `Float`.

I'm using version 2.0 of this package.

issue_title: Anything on the roadmap for a SQLAlchemyClientIDMutation class?
issue_body: I have been using this library for a project and its great so far, however it seems there should also be a class for ClientIDMutations. Is this on the roadmap?

## Problem

Using the `InheritanceManager` on a class causes the following warning:

```
/usr/local/venv/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/django/db/models/fields/related_descriptors.py:157: RemovedInDjango20Warning: use_for_related_fields is deprecated, instead set Meta.base_manager_name on 'my_app.MyModel'.
  qs = self.get_queryset(instance=instance)
```

This seems to be used in a couple of places:
* [`InheritanceManagerMixin`](https://github.com/jazzband/django-model-utils/blob/716e29d83bad90b59373383f9782d05ba089ea8c/model_utils/managers.py#L238)
* [`QueryManagerMixin`](https://github.com/jazzband/django-model-utils/blob/716e29d83bad90b59373383f9782d05ba089ea8c/model_utils/managers.py#L256)

I'm not really sure of the ramifications of just removing this vs. using `Meta.base_manager_name`, etc. I know there's some special things done with the first `Manager` found on a Django `Model` class.

## Environment

- Django Model Utils version: 3.0.0
- Django version:  1.11.5
- Python version: 2.7.6

    error in pyexcel-xls setup command: Invalid environment marker: python_version<"3"

Please then upgrade setuptools to the latest version

tabulate has an argument `headers` which is not set when passing data containing a header row.

Tabulate could autodetect this in some cases (https://bitbucket.org/astanin/python-tabulate/issues/84/detect-headers-firstrow) , but pyexcel-text should be able to do this more reliably.

issue_title: skip_packman isn't a great config name
issue_body:
When a user writes their own artman configuration yaml, normally they will refer to existing file, and many of existing package yaml contains 'skip_packman' for Ruby and Node.

The problem is that nobody outside of our team would understand what packman is, therefore they will copy 'skip_packman: true' line unintentionally.

It's better to rename this.

issue_title: Define BichainDB API v1.1 Client API
issue_body:
Based on the API spec provided by https://github.com/bigchaindb/bigchaindb/issues/362, define the API that the Python client should provide. This should be generic enough such that a [Javascript](https://github.com/bigchaindb/bigchaindb/issues/256) / go / etc. client can be modelled after it.

May be useful to gain inspiration from:

* [elasticsearch-py](https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch-py) (low-level client): [Honza Král - Lessons learned from building Elasticsearch client](https://youtu.be/XoZDdwz43uQ)
* [elastisearch-dsl-py](https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch-dsl-py) (ORM-like client): [Honza Král - Designing a Pythonic Interface](https://youtu.be/FPQrUuddT0s)

issue_title: Symbol stripping on OSX fails for release build
issue_body:
Hi all,

at least since commit 0f294e26ace5350ef14dfa5391ac8b1725061891, there's symbol stripping functionality in the file tools/pybind11Tools.cmake:

```
    ...
      if(CMAKE_STRIP)
        if(APPLE)
          add_custom_command(TARGET ${target_name} POST_BUILD
                             COMMAND ${CMAKE_STRIP} -u -r $<TARGET_FILE:${target_name}>)
        else()
          add_custom_command(TARGET ${target_name} POST_BUILD
                             COMMAND ${CMAKE_STRIP} $<TARGET_FILE:${target_name}>)
        endif()
      endif()
    ...
```
For some reason this step fails for me on OSX version 10.12. What *does* work, is if I execute
```
$ strip -x mylib.so
```
This strips off about 3MB (going from 21MB to 18MB). I'm not an expert on symbol stripping, so I'm not sure what to do in this situation. The stripped library with the `-x` options seems to work fine in practice. The problem has something to do with other libraries that I link against. Here's the full error when I apply the `-u -r` option:

```
/Applications/Xcode.app/Contents/Developer/Toolchains/XcodeDefault.xctoolchain/usr/bin/strip: symbols referenced by indirect symbol table entries that can't be stripped in: /Users/rwols/dev/llvm/build/release/lib/Clara.so
_llvm_regcomp
_llvm_regerror
_llvm_regexec
_llvm_regfree
_llvm_strlcpy
```
I'm using pybind11 in a "tools" project sitting inside LLVM/Clang, so it's hard for me to come up with a minimal example.

I think a solution for now would be to change `-u -r` into `-x` in the `if(APPLE)` block, but I don't know the ramifications of that change. The man page of `strip` on OSX also tells me:
```
For dynamic shared libraries, the maximum level of stripping is usually -x (to remove all non-global symbols).
```

Finally I'd like to note that the stripping functionality as it is now works fine on Ubuntu both for debug and release builds, and it also works on OSX for debug builds. It fails on OSX for release builds.

as discussed on gitter, when I do:

    cmake -DPYTHON_EXECUTABLE:FILEPATH=python2 ..
  
then `make pylint` works but `make check` fails.

If full python path is given, i.e. `-DPYTHON_EXECUTABLE:FILEPATH=/usr/bin/python2`, both work.

BTW it seems that `make check` is not tested on travis.

issue_title: Overriding function that return a reference lead to a compilation error
Exceptions in module initialization should be propagated
issue_body:
## Issue description

Currently, Python exceptions triggered during module initialization cause program termination due to an uncaught `pybind11::error_already_set`.  For example, adding
```
py::object s = py::cast("a"); py::print(s.attr("foo"));
```
to the module initialization code in `python_example/src/main.cpp`, building, and trying to import `python_example` causes the above-mentioned issue.

Compare with adding such code e.g. to the `add` function in the same file; then, calling `add()` results in a normal AttributeError being thrown at the Python level.

Likewise, in pure Python, errors thrown at import time are reported normally (e.g. `a.py`: `import b`; `b.py`: `"a".foo` gives an AttributeError when importing `a`).

If it is technically difficult to rethrow the exception (I am not familiar with the internals of module initialization), it would at least be helpful to reproduce the text (the `__str__`, I guess) of the inner exception to stderr, to help with debugging.

## Reproducible example code

See above.

issue_title: Feature request: combined privkey and fullchain file for jabber servers
issue_body:
At this moment, both [ejabberd](https://github.com/jabberd2/jabberd2/issues/109) and [jabberd2](https://github.com/jabberd2/jabberd2/wiki/InstallGuide-CommonConfigurationTasks#521-assigning-a-certificate-for-use-by-jabber-clients) require private key and certificate to be combined in a single file. Jabberd2 maintainers even explicitly [refused](https://github.com/jabberd2/jabberd2/issues/109) to add an option for separate files and I strongly suspect that with ejabberd it would be the same. I don't have a complete list but I also suspect that there are other pieces of software with similar requirement. So I think it's safe to assume that we do have a use case, albeit slightly specialized one.

It was never a bit deal with certificates valid for a year or longer, just a simple `cat` command very similar to one you need for other servers if you don't have a fullchain in your CA bundle and the same can be used with certbot as a `renew-hook` but I think it would improve learning curve and eliminate human factor better if we had a option (or maybe even default action) instead.

For example, `cat` hook might have a problem with file permissions. Or it can fail for some reason and leave "combined" file not updated without user noticing. Or in case of revokations and deletions it's very likely that user will forget to add a delete-hook (is it even possible?) and invalid certificate will continue to be used. Then cat-ed file will be a real file unlike other four which are usually symlinks which open waay for yet another set of possible inconsistencies. And overall I feel that hooks should be used to reload servers, not for cert files manipulations.

Not to mention that inexperienced users may find it hard to figure out what hooks are and how to use them for this (and option that specifically mentions some of the servers that need it and standard path for the combined file that configuration guides/examples can use are way easier to find and use with less room for error).

So I propose to add an option to certbot that will combine privkey + cert + chain and put it into fifth file alongside the usual privkey, cert, chain, and fullchain. It's a pretty straightforward enhancement that is extremely unlikely to make code worse or break anything. It would be even simpler if we just added a fifth file by default but to that there might be some objections.

I am a python programmer and I can code everything myself and provide a PR but first I want to have a discussion to settle certain key details:

1. What is the best name for the fifth file? combined.pem? keycert.pem? everything.pem? Something else? (Although I need it for jabber servers in particular I think name should be more generic.)

2. What is the best name for the option? `--combine-key-and-cert`?

3. Should it be optional or default? Optional is probably makes more sense as not everybody needs it but default would result in cleaner code and it is pretty straight-forward with few downsides.

4. Is there any particular reason not to implement this at all? I don't see any but maybe I'm missing something.

Hello!

Actually I am a Debian user. Debian stores all (CA, user etv.) certs in `/etc/ssl`.
I think other distros do the same: http://www.tldp.org/LDP/solrhe/Securing-Optimizing-Linux-RH-Edition-v1.3/chap24sec194.html

Would it be possible for certbot to do it also?

People is using CloudFlare, Incapsula, etc. and them can't get Let's Encrypt without [fumbling](https://community.letsencrypt.org/t/how-to-get-a-lets-encrypt-certificate-while-using-cloudflare/6338), so we should add prompt or ```--advanced```, etc. flags for getting certbot-auto for reverse proxies (webroot method).

Like this:
```
Please choose challenge type.
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
1: Normal - Works for basic configurations
2: Advanced - Works also for reverse proxies
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
```
The Normal is the IP-challenge (**tls-sni**) and Advanced is **webroot** file challenge.

issue_title: Failure to parse augeas result with Apache plugin
issue_body:
This was first seen with the certbot 0.10.2 package n Debian 9 ("Stretch"), but I got the same results with 0.11.1 from testing, 0.14.2 from experimental, and 0.16 from github.  This might be a support candidate for #4799, but the fault doesn't actually appear to be in Augeas itself this time.

Certbot was invoked as:

  certbot certonly --apache --cert-name blackbody.resonant.org -d blackbody.resonant.org -d www.resonant.org -d [... lots more ...]

It dies with the output:

  An unexpected error occurred:
  RuntimeError: ('Error during match procedure!', u"/files/etc/apache2/[c]onf.modules.d//*[label()=~regexp('[Vv][Ii][Rr][Tt][Uu][Aa][Ll][Hh][Oo][Ss][Tt]')]")

Full traceback is:

Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/bin/certbot", line 11, in <module>
    load_entry_point('certbot==0.14.2', 'console_scripts', 'certbot')()
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/certbot/main.py", line 742, in main
    return config.func(config, plugins)
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/certbot/main.py", line 662, in certonly
    installer, auth = plug_sel.choose_configurator_plugins(config, plugins, "certonly")
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/certbot/plugins/selection.py", line 187, in choose_configurator_plugins
    installer = pick_installer(config, req_inst, plugins)
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/certbot/plugins/selection.py", line 32, in pick_installer
    config, default, plugins, question, (interfaces.IInstaller,))
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/certbot/plugins/selection.py", line 77, in pick_plugin
    verified.prepare()
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/certbot/plugins/disco.py", line 238, in prepare
    return [plugin_ep.prepare() for plugin_ep in six.itervalues(self._plugins)]
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/certbot/plugins/disco.py", line 120, in prepare
    self._initialized.prepare()
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/certbot_apache/configurator.py", line 196, in prepare
    self.vhosts = self.get_virtual_hosts()
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/certbot_apache/configurator.py", line 611, in get_virtual_hosts
    (vhost_path, parser.case_i("VirtualHost"))))
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/augeas.py", line 415, in match
    raise RuntimeError("Error during match procedure!", path)
RuntimeError: ('Error during match procedure!', u"/files/etc/apache2/[c]onf.modules.d//*[label()=~regexp('[Vv][Ii][Rr][Tt][Uu][Aa][Ll][Hh][Oo][Ss][Tt]')]")


This is, in fact, never going to match anything because Debian doesn't use /etc/apache2/conf.modules.d (that's a RedHat thing), and if I run `augtool print`, I can see a lot of entries being parsed from `/files/etc/apache2/mods-available`, but zero entries matching conf.modules.d.  (Also, why is it looking for virtualhost lines there?  Wouldn't those be in sites-available?)

I could in theory get most of those domains via --webroot instead of --apache, but that's 1) quite tedious with that many domains, and 2) doesn't actually work well for all of them, since some of the domains require authentication starting at the documentroot.

I am greatly looking forward to your wildcard certs, as I also control my own DNS, but since that is 6 months off still, I would greatly appreciate some ideas about how to get this fixed.

Additional weird thing that may or may not be a clue: a Debian Jessie box that I recently upgraded to Stretch was able to run certbot renew without any problems, also using the Apache plugin.  I have absolutely no idea why it breaks on one but not the other.  Both are configured from very similar Puppet rules, and the only major difference is that one has a lot more sites on it, and the one that was able to renew also lacks any augeas entry for conf.modules.d.

issue_body:
Hello, 

I have a classification model which use thulac. I found console would print  `larger than max`  when there are many `?` in text : 
[long.txt](https://github.com/thunlp/THULAC-Python/files/1550054/long.txt)
 
after replace all `?` , this message disappear.  

I think thulac really should add a meaningful message instead of this, it cost some time to find out where it come from.

Right now, I believe the errors detected by PyTA are sorted by their code before being output. However, we should make two modifications:

1. The error code and name should only appear once, and then underneath the individual messages (with line numbers). It should be obvious what message corresponds to the error code, so good use of whitespace there matters.
2. We probably should limit the number of messages shown for a particular error, but show a message saying something like "This error also occurs in XX more places."

@shweta97 I'm assigning you this task since you've been working on the reporter already.

issue_title: Rebalance is not processing after broker restart
issue_body:
In case when broker instance dies, rebalance is added to actions queue, But 'start' action forces rebalance not to process (started rebalance is removed because of start progress), ongoing rebalances are removed during other (or this) broker restart.
Root cause of this bug - 'start' and 'rebalance' actions are added to queue in the same time, (first - rebalance, then - start), then in run() rebalance says that it will stop because there is start in progress, and start just starts.

issue_title: "%bigquery schema --table" produces no visible output on github
issue_body:
github is great for both storing and rendering iPython notebooks.
However for security reasons, [it does not support Javascript](http://blog.jupyter.org/2015/05/07/rendering-notebooks-on-github/).

Thus we see examples like this:

https://github.com/isb-cgc/examples-Python/blob/master/notebooks/Creating%20TCGA%20cohorts%20--%20part%201.ipynb

where `%bigquery schema --table isb-cgc:tcga_201607_beta.Clinical_data` was called, but there is no schema output displayed.

I have worked around this in other contexts with the following:

```
# Import the iPython display function so we can explicitly display dataframes
from IPython.display import display, HTML

# Import Pandas for schema_to_dataframe below
import pandas

# Set up the datalab library for BigQuery access
import datalab.bigquery as bq

# The datalab/bigquery/_schema.py package generates JavaScript HTML output, which
# doesn't render in all environments. Use a dataframe instead.
def schema_to_dataframe(table_name):
  table = bq.Table(table_name)
  rows = []
  for field in table.schema:
    rows.append((field.name, field.data_type, field.mode, field.description))

  return pandas.DataFrame.from_records(rows, columns=['name', 'type', 'mode', 'description'])

def display_dataframe(df):
  # Explicitly generate HTML without the dataframe index
  display(HTML(df.to_html(index=False)))

def display_schema(table_name):
  # Convert the table to a dataframe and explicitly generate HTML without the dataframe index
  display_dataframe(schema_to_dataframe(table_name))
```  

which allows me to call:

`display_schema('project:dataset.table')`

For schema display is there significant value in having script-based output?

As stated in the [corresponding test](https://github.com/SCIP-Interfaces/PySCIPOpt/blob/master/tests/test_linexpr.py#L193), for Python syntax reasons, we would need some parenthesis to express ranged constraints (with both lhs and rhs).

Also, this fact should at least be highlighted in the documentation.

Maybe, we can also catch the exception that is thrown and work around the issue with some Python magic?

Just wanted to put this package on the radar: https://github.com/plangrid/soql

It includes declarative models for objects and some utilities for building SOQL queries from them / loading them with data retrieved via `simple_salesforce`:

```python

from simple_salesforce import Salesforce
from soql import attributes
from soql import load_models_from_salesforce_data
from soql import Model
from soql import select


class Account(Model):
    id = attributes.String('Id')
    deleted = attributes.Boolean('IsDeleted')
    name = attributes.String('Name')
    owner = attributes.Relationship('Owner', related_model=User)
    custom_field = attributes.String('CustomField__c', nullable=True)

class User(Model):
    id = attributes.String('Id')
    email = attributes.String('Email')

sf = Salesforce(...)

query = select(Account) \
    .where(Account.id == '50130000000014c') \
    .join(Account.owner)

resp = sf.query(str(query))

account = load_models_from_salesforce_data(resp)[0]

print(account.id)
print(account.owner.id)
```

If people find this useful, I wonder if its worth considering ways to pull it into `simple_salesforce`?

issue_title: Use `ethGetLogs` to reduce rpc calls from 3 to 1 for API Events
issue_body:
## Problem Definition
API events querying functions now install a filter, query it and uninstal it. We can do better.

## Solution

These 3 RPC calls could be reduced to one by simply using [eth_getlogs](https://github.com/ethereum/wiki/wiki/JSON-RPC#eth_getlogs)

## Tasklist

- [ ] Use `eth_getLogs` instead of the 3 RPC calls.
- [ ] Implement `eth_getLogs` call for rpc client
- [ ] Implement `eth_getLogs` call for tester client
- [ ] Implement `eth_getLogs` call for mock client
- [ ] Use them from inside the proxies

## Problem Definition

The lock must not be removed from the merkle tree.

## Tasks

- [ ] Add a new type to represent a lock that has been unlocked on-chain.
- [ ] On the ContractReceiveChannelWithdraw handle, only unlock  pending locks, and change it to an instance of the new type.

issue_title: Version the database to make breaking testnet upgrades easier
issue_body:

### Tasklist

- [x] Add a constant to define the current version of the database.
- [x] Use the database version as part of the database path (either the path or in the sqllite .db name)
